<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pyproject.toml`

```text
1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["hatchling"]
3	build-backend = "hatchling.build"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "submission-checker"
7	version = "0.1.0"
8	description = "CLI tool for validating MLPerf submissions"
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.10"
11	license = { text = "Apache-2.0" }
12	authors = [{ name = "MLCommons", email = "arav@mlcommons.org" }]
13	dependencies = [
14	    "click>=8.1",
15	    "pydantic>=2.0",
16	    "rich>=13.0",
17	    "pyyaml>=6.0",
18	]
19	
20	[project.scripts]
21	submission-checker = "submission_checker.cli:main"
22	
23	[project.optional-dependencies]
24	dev = [
25	    "pytest>=8.0",
26	    "pytest-cov>=5.0",
27	    "ruff>=0.4",
28	]
29	docs = [
30	    "sphinx>=7.0",
31	    "sphinx-autodoc-typehints>=2.0",
32	    "furo>=2024.1",
33	]
34	
35	[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
36	packages = ["src/submission_checker"]
37	
38	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
39	testpaths = ["tests"]
40	addopts = "--cov=submission_checker --cov-report=term-missing -v"
41	
42	[tool.coverage.run]
43	source = ["src"]
44	
45	[tool.coverage.report]
46	show_missing = true
47	
48	[tool.ruff]
49	src = ["src"]
50	line-length = 100
51	
52	[tool.ruff.lint]
53	select = [
54	    "E",   # pycodestyle errors
55	    "W",   # pycodestyle warnings
56	    "F",   # pyflakes (unused imports, undefined names)
57	    "I",   # isort
58	    "UP",  # pyupgrade (modern Python syntax)
59	    "B",   # flake8-bugbear (likely bugs / bad practices)
60	    "C4",  # flake8-comprehensions (unnecessary comprehension patterns)
61	    "SIM", # flake8-simplify
62	]
63	ignore = [
64	    "SIM108", # ternary operator — sometimes less readable
65	]
```

```text
192	    def _check_sample_accounting(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
193	        """Emit ok/err for the completed + failed == issued invariant (§14).
194	
195	        Skipped when n_samples_issued is 0 (run tool did not track issued count).
196	        """
197	        if s.n_samples_issued > 0:
198	            accounted = s.n_samples_completed + s.n_samples_failed
199	            if accounted != s.n_samples_issued:
200	                self._check_results.append(
201	                    err(
202	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
203	                        f"completed ({s.n_samples_completed}) + failed ({s.n_samples_failed})"
204	                        f" = {accounted} ≠ issued ({s.n_samples_issued})",
205	                        path,
206	                        "#14",
207	                    )
208	                )
209	            else:
210	                self._check_results.append(
211	                    ok(
212	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
213	                        f"Sample accounting consistent: {s.n_samples_issued} issued",
214	                        path,
215	                        "#14",
216	                    )
217	                )
218	
219	    def _check_output_tokens_nonnegative(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
220	        """Emit ok/err for the total_output_tokens >= 0 invariant (§14)."""
221	        if s.total_output_tokens < 0:
222	            self._check_results.append(
223	                err(
224	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
225	                    f"total_output_tokens is negative: {s.total_output_tokens}",
226	                    path,
227	                    "#14",
228	                )
229	            )
230	        else:

232	                ok(
233	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
234	                    f"total_output_tokens={s.total_output_tokens}",
235	                    path,
236	                    "#14",
237	                )
238	            )

240	    @model_validator(mode="after")
241	    def _check_metric_consistency(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunResult:
242	        """§14: validate three run-log accounting invariants via sub-check helpers."""
243	        summary_path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("summary_path")
244	        s = self.summary
245	        self._check_duration_positive(s, summary_path)
246	        self._check_sample_accounting(s, summary_path)
247	        self._check_output_tokens_nonnegative(s, summary_path)
```

```text
*MLPerf Endpoints Rules Task Force — Version 1.0 Draft — 2026-05-05*
```

```text
## 4. Metrics

### 4.1 Primary Metrics

Each measurement point on the pareto curve captures the following metrics at a specific concurrency level:

| Metric | Symbol | Definition |
|---|---|---|
| System Tokens per Second | `system_tps` | Total output tokens produced per second across all concurrent users. `system_tps = total_output_tokens / elapsed_duration_seconds`. |
| TPS per User | `tps_per_user` | Average output tokens per second experienced by a single user. `tps_per_user = system_tps / concurrency`. |
| Time to First Token (P50) | `ttft_p50_ms` | Median time from query issuance to receipt of the first output token, in milliseconds. |
| Time to First Token (P99) | `ttft_p99_ms` | 99th-percentile time to first token, in milliseconds. |
| Concurrency | `concurrency` | The target number of in-flight concurrent queries for this measurement point. |

### 4.2 Derived and Presentation Metrics

The following metrics are derived from primary measurements and used in publication charts:

| Metric | Description |
|---|---|
| **Pareto curve (System TPS vs. TPS/User)** | The primary publication chart. Plots `system_tps` on the y-axis against `tps_per_user` on the x-axis, with each point corresponding to a different concurrency level. Represents the fundamental tradeoff between aggregate system capacity and per-user experience. |
| **Concurrency vs. System TPS** | Shows aggregate throughput scaling with load. Each point annotated with its region. |
| **Concurrency vs. TTFT P50 and P95** | Shows how first-token latency degrades with load. |
| **Concurrency vs. Interactivity** | Shows how interactivity, shown as `tps_per_user`, degrades with load. |

### 4.3 Accuracy Metric

Each benchmark defines a quality target expressed as a minimum acceptable score on the benchmark's accuracy metric (e.g., ROUGE score, exact match, perplexity). The accuracy metric and quality target are specified in the benchmark definition.

One accuracy validation run is required per submission (not per measurement point). The same endpoint configuration, model weights, and software stack used for performance runs must be used for the accuracy run.

---

## 5. Pareto Collection Methodology
--
| **Low Throughput coverage** | ≥ 1 point in the Low Throughput region. | Reject submission. |
| **Medium Throughput coverage** | ≥ 1 point in the Medium Throughput region. | Reject submission. |
| **High Throughput coverage** | ≥ 1 point in the High Throughput region. | Reject submission. |
| **Max concurrency declared** | `M > 32`; declared in `system_desc_id.json`. | Reject submission. |
| **Point cap** | ≤ 32 total measurement points. | Reject points beyond 32. |
| **Concurrency in range** | Each point's concurrency falls within a valid region (including the 10% High Throughput margin), computed using the reference algorithm in [§5.5](#55-region-boundary-reference-algorithm). | Flag out-of-range points. |
| **Load pattern** | All points used `ConcurrencyScheduler`. | Reject non-conforming points. |
| **Run duration** | Each point meets the minimum steady-state duration for its region (see [§6.2](#62-minimum-run-duration)). | Flag non-compliant points. |
| **Minimum query count** | Each point meets the minimum completed queries for its region (see [§6.4](#64-minimum-completed-queries)). | Flag non-compliant points. |
| **Streaming config** | `stream_all_chunks = true` for all performance runs. | Flag non-compliant points. |
| **Metric consistency** | `system_tps` derivable from total tokens and elapsed duration; `tps_per_user = system_tps / concurrency`. | Flag inconsistent points. |
| **Accuracy** | At least one accuracy run passes the benchmark quality target. | Reject submission. |
| **Configuration consistency** | Same model, endpoint configuration, and software stack across all measurement points. | Flag inconsistencies. |

### 9.2 Manual Review Focus Areas

Human reviewers should focus on aspects that automation cannot easily verify:

- Whether the pareto curve shape is physically plausible (throughput should generally increase with concurrency up to saturation, then plateau or decrease).
- Whether metric distributions suggest artificial manipulation (e.g., suspiciously uniform TTFT values across very different concurrency levels).
- Whether the system description accurately reflects the hardware and software used.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-14 14:39:06Z)**_

We **don't fully check it yet.** Here's what exists and what's missing:

**What we have:**

- `RunSummary.system_tps` (`run_result.py:87`) — a computed property that *derives* system_tps as `total_output_tokens / elapsed_s`. This is the formula, but it's only used for reporting, not validated against a stored value.
- `_check_metric_consistency` (`run_result.py:241`) — validates duration > 0, sample accounting, and output tokens ≥ 0 (§14). It does **not** check the tps derivation.

**What's missing (the actual rule from §9.1):**

1. No check that a stored `system_tps` in the result file matches the derivable value `total_output_tokens / elapsed_s`.
2. No `tps_per_user` field or validation that `tps_per_user == system_tps / concurrency`.

So the right place to add these would be as two more sub-check methods alongside `_check_duration_positive` etc., called from `_check_metric_consistency` — checking the stored-vs-derived `system_tps` consistency and the `tps_per_user` identity. Want me to add those?

```text
1	"""Run result models — §8.3 result log schema, accuracy result, and per-run checks."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pydantic import (
8	    BaseModel,
9	    ConfigDict,
10	    Field,
11	    PrivateAttr,
12	    ValidationInfo,
13	    computed_field,
14	    model_validator,
15	)
16	
17	from .regions import MIN_DURATION_MS, classify_concurrency
18	from .results import CheckResult, err, ok, warn
19	from .run_config import RunConfig
20	
21	
22	class PercentileStats(BaseModel):
23	    """Summary statistics dict produced by the endpoints ``compute_summary()`` helper.
24	
25	    Attributes:
26	        total: Sum across all samples (tokens, nanoseconds, etc.).
27	        percentiles: Mapping from percentile string (``"50"``, ``"95"``, …) to value.
28	    """
29	
30	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
31	
32	    total: float = 0.0
33	    percentiles: dict[str, float] = Field(default_factory=dict)
34	
35	
36	class RunSummary(BaseModel):
37	    """Parsed contents of ``results/run_<N>/mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json``.
38	
39	    Follows the endpoints tool ``Report`` msgspec.Struct schema.  Raw timing values
40	    are in nanoseconds; derived millisecond / token-rate fields are computed
41	    properties so that checks can use them without unit conversions.
42	
43	    Attributes:
44	        n_samples_issued: Total queries dispatched to the endpoint.
45	        n_samples_completed: Queries that returned a valid response.
46	        n_samples_failed: Queries that errored or timed out.
47	        duration_ns: Steady-state measurement window length in nanoseconds.
48	        ttft: Time-to-first-token statistics (nanoseconds).
49	        output_sequence_lengths: Output token count statistics.
50	    """
51	
52	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
53	
54	    n_samples_issued: int = 0
55	    n_samples_completed: int
56	    n_samples_failed: int = 0
57	    duration_ns: float
58	    ttft: PercentileStats = Field(default_factory=PercentileStats)
59	    output_sequence_lengths: PercentileStats = Field(default_factory=PercentileStats)
60	
61	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
62	    @property
63	    def duration_ms(self) -> float:
64	        """Measurement duration in milliseconds."""
65	        return self.duration_ns / 1_000_000
66	
67	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
68	    @property
69	    def sample_count(self) -> int:
70	        """Alias for ``n_samples_completed``."""
71	        return self.n_samples_completed
72	
73	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
74	    @property
75	    def total_output_tokens(self) -> int:
76	        """Total output tokens from ``output_sequence_lengths.total``."""
77	        return int(self.output_sequence_lengths.total)
78	
79	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
80	    @property
81	    def elapsed_duration_seconds(self) -> float:
82	        """Measurement duration in seconds."""
83	        return self.duration_ns / 1e9
84	
85	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
86	    @property
87	    def system_tps(self) -> float:
88	        """System-wide tokens per second: ``total_output_tokens / elapsed_s``."""
89	        elapsed_s = self.elapsed_duration_seconds
90	        return self.total_output_tokens / elapsed_s if elapsed_s > 0 else 0.0
91	
92	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
93	    @property
94	    def ttft_p50_ms(self) -> float:
95	        """Median time to first token in milliseconds."""
96	        return self.ttft.percentiles.get("50", 0.0) / 1_000_000
97	
98	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
99	    @property
100	    def ttft_p95_ms(self) -> float:
101	        """95th-percentile time to first token in milliseconds."""
102	        return self.ttft.percentiles.get("95", 0.0) / 1_000_000
103	
104	
105	class AccuracyResult(BaseModel):
106	    """Parsed contents of ``accuracy/accuracy_result.json`` (§4.3, §6.6).
107	
108	    Attributes:
109	        metric: Name of the accuracy metric (e.g., ``rouge1``).
110	        score: Achieved score on the accuracy dataset.
111	        quality_target: Minimum acceptable score defined by the benchmark.
112	        passed: Whether the score meets the quality target.
113	    """
114	
115	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
116	
117	    metric: str
118	    score: float
119	    quality_target: float
120	    passed: bool
121	
122	
123	class RunResult(BaseModel):
124	    """Paired config and result summary for one measurement run."""
125	
126	    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
127	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
128	
129	    config: RunConfig
130	    summary: RunSummary
131	    yaml_path: Path
132	
133	    @model_validator(mode="after")
134	    def _check_run_duration(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunResult:
135	        """§11: warn when measured duration is below the per-region minimum."""
136	        regions = (info.context or {}).get("regions")
137	        summary_path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("summary_path")
138	        if regions is None:
139	            return self
140	        c = self.config.concurrency
141	        region = classify_concurrency(c, regions)
142	        if region is None:
143	            return self  # already flagged by concurrency-in-range
144	        min_ms = MIN_DURATION_MS.get(region, 0)
145	        duration_ms = self.summary.duration_ms
146	        if duration_ms < min_ms:
147	            self._check_results.append(
148	                warn(
149	                    "run-duration",
150	                    f"Run {c} ({region}): duration {duration_ms:.0f} ms < minimum {min_ms} ms"
151	                    " (§6.2 values pending WG ratification)",
152	                    summary_path,
153	                    "#11",
154	                )
155	            )
156	        else:
157	            self._check_results.append(
158	                ok(
159	                    "run-duration",
160	                    f"Run {c}: duration {duration_ms:.0f} ms meets minimum for {region}",
161	                    summary_path,
162	                    "#11",
163	                )
164	            )
165	        return self
166	
167	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
168	    # Metric-consistency sub-checks (called from _check_metric_consistency)
169	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
170	
171	    def _check_duration_positive(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
172	        """Emit ok/err for the duration_ns > 0 invariant (§14)."""
173	        if s.duration_ns <= 0:
174	            self._check_results.append(
175	                err(
176	                    "metric-consistency-duration",
177	                    f"duration_ns is not positive: {s.duration_ns}",
178	                    path,
179	                    "#14",
180	                )
181	            )
182	        else:
183	            self._check_results.append(
184	                ok(
185	                    "metric-consistency-duration",
186	                    f"duration_ns={s.duration_ns:.0f} ns",
187	                    path,
188	                    "#14",
189	                )
190	            )
191	
192	    def _check_sample_accounting(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
193	        """Emit ok/err for the completed + failed == issued invariant (§14).
194	
195	        Skipped when n_samples_issued is 0 (run tool did not track issued count).
196	        """
197	        if s.n_samples_issued > 0:
198	            accounted = s.n_samples_completed + s.n_samples_failed
199	            if accounted != s.n_samples_issued:
200	                self._check_results.append(
201	                    err(
202	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
203	                        f"completed ({s.n_samples_completed}) + failed ({s.n_samples_failed})"
204	                        f" = {accounted} ≠ issued ({s.n_samples_issued})",
205	                        path,
206	                        "#14",
207	                    )
208	                )
209	            else:
210	                self._check_results.append(
211	                    ok(
212	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
213	                        f"Sample accounting consistent: {s.n_samples_issued} issued",
214	                        path,
215	                        "#14",
216	                    )
217	                )
218	
219	    def _check_output_tokens_nonnegative(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
220	        """Emit ok/err for the total_output_tokens >= 0 invariant (§14)."""
221	        if s.total_output_tokens < 0:
222	            self._check_results.append(
223	                err(
224	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
225	                    f"total_output_tokens is negative: {s.total_output_tokens}",
226	                    path,
227	                    "#14",
228	                )
229	            )
230	        else:
231	            self._check_results.append(
232	                ok(
233	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
234	                    f"total_output_tokens={s.total_output_tokens}",
235	                    path,
236	                    "#14",
237	                )
238	            )
239	
240	    @model_validator(mode="after")
241	    def _check_metric_consistency(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunResult:
242	        """§14: validate three run-log accounting invariants via sub-check helpers."""
243	        summary_path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("summary_path")
244	        s = self.summary
245	        self._check_duration_positive(s, summary_path)
246	        self._check_sample_accounting(s, summary_path)
247	        self._check_output_tokens_nonnegative(s, summary_path)
248	        return self
249
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
80	    )
```

```text
83	def _summary(
84	    n_completed: int = 1000,
85	    n_issued: int = 1000,
86	    n_failed: int = 0,
87	    duration_ns: float = 1_200_000_000_000.0,

99	
100	def _passed(results: list[CheckResult]) -> bool:
101	    return all(r.severity != Severity.ERROR for r in results)
102	
103	
104	def _model_ctx(
105	    tmp_path: Path,
106	    all_run_count: int = 7,
107	    valid_runs: list[tuple[Path, RunConfig]] | None = None,
108	    loaded_results: list[tuple[RunConfig, RunSummary]] | None = None,
109	    system_desc: SystemDescription | None = None,
110	    model_name: str = "llama3-70b",
111	    accuracy_result: AccuracyResult | None = None,
112	) -> ModelContext:
113	    model_dir = tmp_path / model_name
114	    model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
115	    (model_dir / "runs").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
116	    (model_dir / "results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
117	    (model_dir / "accuracy").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
118	    return ModelContext(
119	        system_id="test-sys",
120	        system_desc=system_desc or _system_desc(benchmark_model=model_name),
121	        model_dir=model_dir,
122	        regions=_REGIONS,
123	        runs_dir=model_dir / "runs",
124	        accuracy_dir=model_dir / "accuracy",
125	        all_run_count=all_run_count,
126	        valid_runs=valid_runs or [],
127	        loaded_results=loaded_results or [],
128	        accuracy_result=accuracy_result,
129	    )
130
```

```text
### **1. Submission completeness — all required files/dirs/YAML/results/system descriptions present**
- **FOUND**: `/home/arav/MLPerf/endpoints-submission-checker/src/submission_checker/src/structure.py`, lines 12-147

- **FOUND**: `/home/arav/MLPerf/endpoints-submission-checker/src/submission_checker/src/checker.py`, lines 95-105, 195-205
  - Checks for presence of `*.json` files in `systems/` (lines 95-105)
  - Checks for presence of `run_*.yaml` files in `runs/` directory (lines 195-205)
  - Check rules: `"required-dir"`, `"src-dir"`, `"pareto-dir-exists"`, `"pareto-subdir"`, `"measurement-runs-present"`

### **2. Point count — ≥ 7 total measurement points**
- **FOUND**: `/home/arav/MLPerf/endpoints-submission-checker/src/submission_checker/src/models/context.py`, lines 36-57
  - `_check_run_count()` validator at line 37

### **3. Low Latency coverage — ≥ 1 point with concurrency in [1, 32]**
- **FOUND**: `/home/arav/MLPerf/endpoints-submission-checker/src/submission_checker/src/models/context.py`, lines 59-90
```

```text
1	"""Submission checker — orchestrates §9.1 automated compliance checks.
2	
3	Loading and structural validation live here; all rule logic lives in Pydantic
4	model validators on RunConfig, RunResult, and ModelContext.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
10	
11	from .loader import (
12	    load_accuracy_result,
13	    load_result_summary,
14	    load_run_config,
15	    load_system_description,
16	)
17	from .models import (
18	    CheckResult,
19	    ModelContext,
20	    Regions,
21	    Report,
22	    RunConfig,
23	    RunResult,
24	    RunSummary,
25	    Severity,
26	    SystemDescription,
27	    compute_regions,
28	)
29	from .models import err as _err
30	from .models import ok as _ok
31	from .models import warn as _warn
32	from .structure import ModelDir, SrcDir, SubmissionDir, SystemPareto
33	
34	if TYPE_CHECKING:
35	    from pathlib import Path
36	
37	
38	class SubmissionChecker:
39	    """Validates an MLPerf Endpoints submission directory against §9.1 rules.
40	
41	    The *submission_path* should be the submitting organisation's root directory,
42	    which must contain ``systems/`` and ``pareto/`` subdirectories as specified
43	    in §8.1.
44	
45	    Args:
46	        submission_path: Root directory of the submission to validate.
47	
48	    Example::
49	
50	        checker = SubmissionChecker(Path("/submissions/acme_corp"))
51	        report = checker.run()
52	        for err in report.errors:
53	            print(err.rule, err.message)
54	    """
55	
56	    def __init__(self, submission_path: Path) -> None:
57	        self.submission_path = submission_path
58	
59	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
60	    # Public entry point
61	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	    def run(self) -> Report:
64	        """Run all §9.1 automated checks and return an aggregated
65	        :class:`~submission_checker.models.Report`.
66	
67	        Returns:
68	            A :class:`~submission_checker.models.Report` with every
69	            :class:`~submission_checker.models.CheckResult` produced.
70	        """
71	        report = Report(submission_path=self.submission_path)
72	
73	        if not self.submission_path.exists():
74	            report.results.append(
75	                _err(
76	                    "path-exists",
77	                    f"Submission path does not exist: {self.submission_path}",
78	                    self.submission_path,
79	                    "#1",
80	                )
81	            )
82	            return report
83	        report.results.append(
84	            _ok("path-exists", "Submission path exists", self.submission_path, "#1")
85	        )
86	
87	        submission_dir = SubmissionDir(root=self.submission_path)
88	        report.results.extend(submission_dir._check_results)
89	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in submission_dir._check_results):
90	            return report
91	
92	        systems_dir = submission_dir.systems_dir
93	        pareto_dir = submission_dir.pareto_dir
94	
95	        system_jsons = sorted(systems_dir.glob("*.json"))
96	        if not system_jsons:
97	            report.results.append(
98	                _err(
99	                    "system-description-present",
100	                    "No *.json files found in systems/",
101	                    systems_dir,
102	                    "#1",
103	                )
104	            )
105	            return report
106	
107	        for system_json in system_jsons:
108	            report.results.extend(self._check_system(system_json, pareto_dir))
109	
110	        return report
111	
112	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
113	    # Per-system orchestration
114	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
115	
116	    def _check_system(self, system_json: Path, pareto_dir: Path) -> list[CheckResult]:
117	        results: list[CheckResult] = []
118	        system_id = system_json.stem
119	
120	        system_desc, load_err = load_system_description(system_json)
121	        if load_err:
122	            results.append(_err("system-description-valid", load_err, system_json, "#1"))
123	            return results
124	        assert system_desc is not None
125	        results.append(
126	            _ok(
127	                "system-description-valid",
128	                f"System description valid: {system_id}",
129	                system_json,
130	                "#1",
131	            )
132	        )
133	
134	        M = system_desc.max_supported_concurrency
135	        results.append(
136	            _ok("max-concurrency-declared", f"max_supported_concurrency = {M}", system_json, "#7")
137	        )
138	
139	        try:
140	            regions = compute_regions(M)
141	        except ValueError as exc:
142	            results.append(_err("region-computation", str(exc), system_json, "#7"))
143	            return results
144	
145	        src = SrcDir(root=self.submission_path, division=system_desc.division)
146	        results.extend(src._check_results)
147	
148	        system_pareto = SystemPareto(pareto_dir=pareto_dir, system_id=system_id)
149	        results.extend(system_pareto._check_results)
150	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in system_pareto._check_results):
151	            return results
152	        system_pareto_dir = system_pareto.system_dir
153	        model_dirs = [d for d in sorted(system_pareto_dir.iterdir()) if d.is_dir()]
154	        if not model_dirs:
155	            results.append(
156	                _err(
157	                    "benchmark-model-dir",
158	                    f"No benchmark-model directories in pareto/{system_id}/",
159	                    system_pareto_dir,
160	                    "#1",
161	                )
162	            )
163	            return results
164	
165	        for model_dir in model_dirs:
166	            results.extend(self._check_model(system_id, system_desc, regions, model_dir))
167	
168	        return results
169	
170	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
171	    # Per benchmark-model orchestration
172	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
173	
174	    def _check_model(
175	        self,
176	        system_id: str,
177	        system_desc: SystemDescription,
178	        regions: Regions,
179	        model_dir: Path,
180	    ) -> list[CheckResult]:
181	        results: list[CheckResult] = []
182	        benchmark_model = model_dir.name
183	
184	        model_structure = ModelDir(
185	            root=model_dir, system_id=system_id, benchmark_model=benchmark_model
186	        )
187	        results.extend(model_structure._check_results)
188	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in model_structure._check_results):
189	            return results
190	
191	        runs_dir = model_structure.runs_dir
192	        results_dir = model_structure.results_dir
193	        accuracy_dir = model_structure.accuracy_dir
194	
195	        run_yamls = sorted(runs_dir.glob("run_*.yaml"))
196	        if not run_yamls:
197	            results.append(
198	                _err(
199	                    "measurement-runs-present",
200	                    f"No run_*.yaml files in {runs_dir.relative_to(self.submission_path)}",
201	                    runs_dir,
202	                    "#1",
203	                )
204	            )
205	            return results
206	
207	        valid_runs: list[tuple[Path, RunConfig]] = []
208	        loaded_results: list[tuple[RunConfig, RunSummary]] = []
209	
210	        for yaml_path in run_yamls:
211	            config, config_results = load_run_config(
212	                yaml_path, context={"regions": regions, "yaml_path": yaml_path}
213	            )
214	            results.extend(config_results)
215	            if config is None:
216	                continue
217	
218	            # filename-concurrency consistency warning
219	            try:
220	                fname_concurrency = int(yaml_path.stem.split("_")[1])
221	                if fname_concurrency != config.concurrency:
222	                    results.append(
223	                        _warn(
224	                            "run-filename-concurrency",
225	                            f"{yaml_path.name}: filename concurrency {fname_concurrency}"
226	                            f" ≠ declared {config.concurrency}",
227	                            yaml_path,
228	                            "#1",
229	                        )
230	                    )
231	            except (IndexError, ValueError):
232	                pass
233	
234	            valid_runs.append((yaml_path, config))
235	
236	            summary_path = (
237	                results_dir / f"run_{config.concurrency}" / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json"
238	            )
239	            if not summary_path.exists():
240	                results.append(
241	                    _err(
242	                        "result-file-present",
243	                        f"Missing result log for run_{config.concurrency}:"
244	                        f" {summary_path.relative_to(self.submission_path)}",
245	                        summary_path,
246	                        "#1",
247	                    )
248	                )
249	                continue
250	
251	            summary, load_err = load_result_summary(summary_path)
252	            if load_err:
253	                results.append(_err("result-file-valid", load_err, summary_path, "#1"))
254	                continue
255	
256	            # RunResult validates run-duration and metric-consistency
257	            run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
258	                {"config": config, "summary": summary, "yaml_path": yaml_path},
259	                context={"regions": regions, "summary_path": summary_path},
260	            )
261	            results.extend(run_result._check_results)
262	            loaded_results.append((config, summary))
263	
264	        # Load accuracy
265	        accuracy_result = None
266	        txt_path = accuracy_dir / "accuracy.txt"
267	        if not txt_path.exists():
268	            results.append(_err("accuracy-file", "Missing accuracy/accuracy.txt", txt_path, "#15"))
269	        json_path = accuracy_dir / "accuracy_result.json"
270	        if not json_path.exists():
271	            results.append(
272	                _err("accuracy-file", "Missing accuracy/accuracy_result.json", json_path, "#15")
273	            )
274	        else:
275	            accuracy_result, acc_err = load_accuracy_result(json_path)
276	            if acc_err:
277	                results.append(_err("accuracy-valid", acc_err, json_path, "#15"))
278	                accuracy_result = None
279	
280	        # ModelContext validates run-count, regional-coverage, config-consistency, accuracy-gate
281	        model_ctx = ModelContext(
282	            system_id=system_id,
283	            system_desc=system_desc,
284	            model_dir=model_dir,
285	            regions=regions,
286	            runs_dir=runs_dir,
287	            accuracy_dir=accuracy_dir,
288	            all_run_count=len(run_yamls),
289	            valid_runs=valid_runs,
290	            loaded_results=loaded_results,
291	            accuracy_result=accuracy_result,
292	        )
293	        results.extend(model_ctx._check_results)
294	
295	        return results
296
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""Pydantic models for submission directory structure validation (§8.1)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pydantic import BaseModel, PrivateAttr, computed_field, model_validator
8	
9	from .models import CheckResult, Division, err, ok
10	
11	
12	class SubmissionDir(BaseModel):
13	    """Validates the top-level submission directory: systems/ and pareto/ must exist."""
14	
15	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
16	
17	    root: Path
18	
19	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
20	    @property
21	    def systems_dir(self) -> Path:
22	        return self.root / "systems"
23	
24	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
25	    @property
26	    def pareto_dir(self) -> Path:
27	        return self.root / "pareto"
28	
29	    @model_validator(mode="after")
30	    def _check_required_dirs(self) -> SubmissionDir:
31	        for name in ("systems", "pareto"):
32	            path = self.root / name
33	            if path.is_dir():
34	                self._check_results.append(
35	                    ok("required-dir", f"Found required directory: {name}/", path, "#1")
36	                )
37	            else:
38	                self._check_results.append(
39	                    err("required-dir", f"Missing required directory: {name}/", path, "#1")
40	                )
41	        return self
42	
43	
44	class SrcDir(BaseModel):
45	    """Validates src/ exists for Standardized division submissions (§2.2.1)."""
46	
47	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
48	
49	    root: Path
50	    division: Division
51	
52	    @model_validator(mode="after")
53	    def _check_src(self) -> SrcDir:
54	        if self.division != Division.STANDARDIZED:
55	            return self
56	        src_dir = self.root / "src"
57	        if src_dir.is_dir():
58	            self._check_results.append(
59	                ok("src-dir", "src/ present (required for Standardized division)", src_dir, "#1")
60	            )
61	        else:
62	            self._check_results.append(
63	                err(
64	                    "src-dir",
65	                    "Missing src/ directory (required for Standardized division)",
66	                    src_dir,
67	                    "#1",
68	                )
69	            )
70	        return self
71	
72	
73	class SystemPareto(BaseModel):
74	    """Validates pareto/<system_id>/ exists."""
75	
76	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
77	
78	    pareto_dir: Path
79	    system_id: str
80	
81	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
82	    @property
83	    def system_dir(self) -> Path:
84	        return self.pareto_dir / self.system_id
85	
86	    @model_validator(mode="after")
87	    def _check_dir_exists(self) -> SystemPareto:
88	        path = self.pareto_dir / self.system_id
89	        if path.is_dir():
90	            self._check_results.append(
91	                ok("pareto-dir-exists", f"Found pareto/{self.system_id}/", path, "#1")
92	            )
93	        else:
94	            self._check_results.append(
95	                err("pareto-dir-exists", f"No pareto/{self.system_id}/ directory found", path, "#1")
96	            )
97	        return self
98	
99	
100	class ModelDir(BaseModel):
101	    """Validates runs/, results/, and accuracy/ exist under a benchmark-model directory."""
102	
103	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
104	
105	    root: Path
106	    system_id: str
107	    benchmark_model: str
108	
109	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
110	    @property
111	    def runs_dir(self) -> Path:
112	        return self.root / "runs"
113	
114	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
115	    @property
116	    def results_dir(self) -> Path:
117	        return self.root / "results"
118	
119	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
120	    @property
121	    def accuracy_dir(self) -> Path:
122	        return self.root / "accuracy"
123	
124	    @model_validator(mode="after")
125	    def _check_subdirs(self) -> ModelDir:
126	        for name in ("runs", "results", "accuracy"):
127	            path = self.root / name
128	            if path.is_dir():
129	                self._check_results.append(
130	                    ok(
131	                        "pareto-subdir",
132	                        f"Found {name}/ in pareto/{self.system_id}/{self.benchmark_model}/",
133	                        path,
134	                        "#1",
135	                    )
136	                )
137	            else:
138	                self._check_results.append(
139	                    err(
140	                        "pareto-subdir",
141	                        f"Missing {name}/ in pareto/{self.system_id}/{self.benchmark_model}/",
142	                        path,
143	                        "#1",
144	                    )
145	                )
146	        return self
147
```

```text
1	"""Model context — aggregate model-level validation (run count, coverage, consistency, accuracy)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, PrivateAttr, model_validator
8	
9	from .regions import Regions
10	from .results import CheckResult, err, ok, warn
11	from .run_config import RunConfig
12	from .run_result import AccuracyResult, RunSummary
13	from .system import SystemDescription
14	
15	_MIN_RUNS = 7
16	_MAX_RUNS = 32
17	
18	
19	class ModelContext(BaseModel):
20	    """Aggregated data for one benchmark-model directory — carries all model-level validation."""
21	
22	    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
23	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
24	
25	    system_id: str
26	    system_desc: SystemDescription
27	    model_dir: Path
28	    regions: Regions
29	    runs_dir: Path
30	    accuracy_dir: Path
31	    all_run_count: int
32	    valid_runs: list[tuple[Path, RunConfig]]
33	    loaded_results: list[tuple[RunConfig, RunSummary]]
34	    accuracy_result: AccuracyResult | None = None
35	
36	    @model_validator(mode="after")
37	    def _check_run_count(self) -> ModelContext:
38	        """§2, §8: submission must have 7–32 measurement runs."""
39	        n = self.all_run_count
40	        if n < _MIN_RUNS:
41	            self._check_results.append(
42	                err(
43	                    "run-count",
44	                    f"Only {n} measurement run(s) — minimum {_MIN_RUNS} required",
45	                    self.runs_dir,
46	                    "#2, #8",
47	                )
48	            )
49	        else:
50	            self._check_results.append(
51	                ok("run-count", f"Run count OK: {n}", self.runs_dir, "#2, #8")
52	            )
53	        if n > _MAX_RUNS:
54	            self._check_results.append(
55	                err("run-cap", f"{n} runs exceed the {_MAX_RUNS}-run cap", self.runs_dir, "#2, #8")
56	            )
57	        return self
58	
59	    @model_validator(mode="after")
60	    def _check_regional_coverage(self) -> ModelContext:
61	        """§3–6: at least one valid run must fall in each of the four concurrency regions."""
62	        concurrencies = [config.concurrency for _, config in self.valid_runs]
63	        r = self.regions
64	        coverage_checks = [
65	            ("low-latency-coverage", "Low Latency", r.low_latency),
66	            ("low-throughput-coverage", "Low Throughput", r.low_throughput),
67	            ("med-throughput-coverage", "Medium Throughput", r.med_throughput),
68	            ("high-throughput-coverage", "High Throughput", r.high_throughput),
69	        ]
70	        for rule, label, bounds in coverage_checks:
71	            matching = [c for c in concurrencies if bounds.contains(c)]
72	            if matching:
73	                self._check_results.append(
74	                    ok(
75	                        rule,
76	                        f"{label} region covered: {sorted(matching)} (range {bounds})",
77	                        self.runs_dir,
78	                        "#3–6",
79	                    )
80	                )
81	            else:
82	                self._check_results.append(
83	                    err(
84	                        rule,
85	                        f"No run in {label} region (concurrency {bounds})",
86	                        self.runs_dir,
87	                        "#3–6",
88	                    )
89	                )
90	        return self
91	
92	    @model_validator(mode="after")
93	    def _check_config_consistency(self) -> ModelContext:
94	        """§16: all runs must use the same dataset; directory name must match benchmark_model."""
95	        if not self.loaded_results:
96	            return self
97	        datasets = {config.dataset for config, _ in self.loaded_results}
98	        if len(datasets) > 1:
99	            self._check_results.append(
100	                err(
101	                    "config-consistency-dataset",
102	                    f"Inconsistent datasets across runs: {datasets}",
103	                    self.model_dir,
104	                    "#16",
105	                )
106	            )
107	        else:
108	            self._check_results.append(
109	                ok(
110	                    "config-consistency-dataset",
111	                    f"Dataset consistent: {next(iter(datasets))}",
112	                    self.model_dir,
113	                    "#16",
114	                )
115	            )
116	
117	        if self.model_dir.name != self.system_desc.benchmark_model:
118	            self._check_results.append(
119	                warn(
120	                    "config-consistency-model",
121	                    f"Directory name '{self.model_dir.name}' ≠"
122	                    f" system_desc benchmark_model '{self.system_desc.benchmark_model}'",
123	                    self.model_dir,
124	                    "#16",
125	                )
126	            )
127	        else:
128	            self._check_results.append(
129	                ok(
130	                    "config-consistency-model",
131	                    f"Benchmark model consistent: {self.model_dir.name}",
132	                    self.model_dir,
133	                    "#16",
134	                )
135	            )
136	        return self
137	
138	    @model_validator(mode="after")
139	    def _check_accuracy(self) -> ModelContext:
140	        """§15: accuracy score must meet or exceed the benchmark quality_target."""
141	        if self.accuracy_result is None:
142	            return self  # file missing/invalid already reported by checker.py
143	        accuracy = self.accuracy_result
144	        json_path = self.accuracy_dir / "accuracy_result.json"
145	        if accuracy.passed:
146	            self._check_results.append(
147	                ok(
148	                    "accuracy-gate",
149	                    f"Accuracy gate PASSED: {accuracy.metric} = {accuracy.score:.4f}"
150	                    f" ≥ target {accuracy.quality_target:.4f}",
151	                    json_path,
152	                    "#15",
153	                )
154	            )
155	        else:
156	            self._check_results.append(
157	                err(
158	                    "accuracy-gate",
159	                    f"Accuracy gate FAILED: {accuracy.metric} = {accuracy.score:.4f}"
160	                    f" < target {accuracy.quality_target:.4f}",
161	                    json_path,
162	                    "#15",
163	                )
164	            )
165	        return self
166
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""Run result models — §8.3 result log schema, accuracy result, and per-run checks."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pydantic import (
8	    BaseModel,
9	    ConfigDict,
10	    Field,
11	    PrivateAttr,
12	    ValidationInfo,
13	    computed_field,
14	    model_validator,
15	)
16	
17	from .regions import MIN_DURATION_MS, classify_concurrency
18	from .results import CheckResult, err, ok, warn
19	from .run_config import RunConfig
20	
21	
22	class PercentileStats(BaseModel):
23	    """Summary statistics dict produced by the endpoints ``compute_summary()`` helper.
24	
25	    Attributes:
26	        total: Sum across all samples (tokens, nanoseconds, etc.).
27	        percentiles: Mapping from percentile string (``"50"``, ``"95"``, …) to value.
28	    """
29	
30	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
31	
32	    total: float = 0.0
33	    percentiles: dict[str, float] = Field(default_factory=dict)
34	
35	
36	class RunSummary(BaseModel):
37	    """Parsed contents of ``results/run_<N>/mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json``.
38	
39	    Follows the endpoints tool ``Report`` msgspec.Struct schema.  Raw timing values
40	    are in nanoseconds; derived millisecond / token-rate fields are computed
41	    properties so that checks can use them without unit conversions.
42	
43	    Attributes:
44	        n_samples_issued: Total queries dispatched to the endpoint.
45	        n_samples_completed: Queries that returned a valid response.
46	        n_samples_failed: Queries that errored or timed out.
47	        duration_ns: Steady-state measurement window length in nanoseconds.
48	        ttft: Time-to-first-token statistics (nanoseconds).
49	        output_sequence_lengths: Output token count statistics.
50	    """
51	
52	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
53	
54	    n_samples_issued: int = 0
55	    n_samples_completed: int
56	    n_samples_failed: int = 0
57	    duration_ns: float
58	    ttft: PercentileStats = Field(default_factory=PercentileStats)
59	    output_sequence_lengths: PercentileStats = Field(default_factory=PercentileStats)
60	
61	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
62	    @property
63	    def duration_ms(self) -> float:
64	        """Measurement duration in milliseconds."""
65	        return self.duration_ns / 1_000_000
66	
67	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
68	    @property
69	    def sample_count(self) -> int:
70	        """Alias for ``n_samples_completed``."""
71	        return self.n_samples_completed
72	
73	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
74	    @property
75	    def total_output_tokens(self) -> int:
76	        """Total output tokens from ``output_sequence_lengths.total``."""
77	        return int(self.output_sequence_lengths.total)
78	
79	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
80	    @property
81	    def elapsed_duration_seconds(self) -> float:
82	        """Measurement duration in seconds."""
83	        return self.duration_ns / 1e9
84	
85	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
86	    @property
87	    def system_tps(self) -> float:
88	        """System-wide tokens per second: ``total_output_tokens / elapsed_s``."""
89	        elapsed_s = self.elapsed_duration_seconds
90	        return self.total_output_tokens / elapsed_s if elapsed_s > 0 else 0.0
91	
92	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
93	    @property
94	    def ttft_p50_ms(self) -> float:
95	        """Median time to first token in milliseconds."""
96	        return self.ttft.percentiles.get("50", 0.0) / 1_000_000
97	
98	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
99	    @property
100	    def ttft_p95_ms(self) -> float:
101	        """95th-percentile time to first token in milliseconds."""
102	        return self.ttft.percentiles.get("95", 0.0) / 1_000_000
103	
104	
105	class AccuracyResult(BaseModel):
106	    """Parsed contents of ``accuracy/accuracy_result.json`` (§4.3, §6.6).
107	
108	    Attributes:
109	        metric: Name of the accuracy metric (e.g., ``rouge1``).
110	        score: Achieved score on the accuracy dataset.
111	        quality_target: Minimum acceptable score defined by the benchmark.
112	        passed: Whether the score meets the quality target.
113	    """
114	
115	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
116	
117	    metric: str
118	    score: float
119	    quality_target: float
120	    passed: bool
121	
122	
123	_TPS_TOLERANCE = 0.01  # 1% relative tolerance for stored-vs-derived comparisons
124	
125	
126	class RunResult(BaseModel):
127	    """Paired config and result summary for one measurement run."""
128	
129	    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
130	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
131	
132	    config: RunConfig
133	    summary: RunSummary
134	    yaml_path: Path
135	
136	    @model_validator(mode="after")
137	    def _check_run_duration(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunResult:
138	        """§11: warn when measured duration is below the per-region minimum."""
139	        regions = (info.context or {}).get("regions")
140	        summary_path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("summary_path")
141	        if regions is None:
142	            return self
143	        c = self.config.concurrency
144	        region = classify_concurrency(c, regions)
145	        if region is None:
146	            return self  # already flagged by concurrency-in-range
147	        min_ms = MIN_DURATION_MS.get(region, 0)
148	        duration_ms = self.summary.duration_ms
149	        if duration_ms < min_ms:
150	            self._check_results.append(
151	                warn(
152	                    "run-duration",
153	                    f"Run {c} ({region}): duration {duration_ms:.0f} ms < minimum {min_ms} ms"
154	                    " (§6.2 values pending WG ratification)",
155	                    summary_path,
156	                    "#11",
157	                )
158	            )
159	        else:
160	            self._check_results.append(
161	                ok(
162	                    "run-duration",
163	                    f"Run {c}: duration {duration_ms:.0f} ms meets minimum for {region}",
164	                    summary_path,
165	                    "#11",
166	                )
167	            )
168	        return self
169	
170	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
171	    # Metric-consistency sub-checks (called from _check_metric_consistency)
172	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
173	
174	    def _check_duration_positive(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
175	        """Emit ok/err for the duration_ns > 0 invariant (§14)."""
176	        if s.duration_ns <= 0:
177	            self._check_results.append(
178	                err(
179	                    "metric-consistency-duration",
180	                    f"duration_ns is not positive: {s.duration_ns}",
181	                    path,
182	                    "#14",
183	                )
184	            )
185	        else:
186	            self._check_results.append(
187	                ok(
188	                    "metric-consistency-duration",
189	                    f"duration_ns={s.duration_ns:.0f} ns",
190	                    path,
191	                    "#14",
192	                )
193	            )
194	
195	    def _check_sample_accounting(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
196	        """Emit ok/err for the completed + failed == issued invariant (§14).
197	
198	        Skipped when n_samples_issued is 0 (run tool did not track issued count).
199	        """
200	        if s.n_samples_issued > 0:
201	            accounted = s.n_samples_completed + s.n_samples_failed
202	            if accounted != s.n_samples_issued:
203	                self._check_results.append(
204	                    err(
205	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
206	                        f"completed ({s.n_samples_completed}) + failed ({s.n_samples_failed})"
207	                        f" = {accounted} ≠ issued ({s.n_samples_issued})",
208	                        path,
209	                        "#14",
210	                    )
211	                )
212	            else:
213	                self._check_results.append(
214	                    ok(
215	                        "metric-consistency-accounting",
216	                        f"Sample accounting consistent: {s.n_samples_issued} issued",
217	                        path,
218	                        "#14",
219	                    )
220	                )
221	
222	    def _check_output_tokens_nonnegative(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
223	        """Emit ok/err for the total_output_tokens >= 0 invariant (§14)."""
224	        if s.total_output_tokens < 0:
225	            self._check_results.append(
226	                err(
227	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
228	                    f"total_output_tokens is negative: {s.total_output_tokens}",
229	                    path,
230	                    "#14",
231	                )
232	            )
233	        else:
234	            self._check_results.append(
235	                ok(
236	                    "metric-consistency-output-tokens",
237	                    f"total_output_tokens={s.total_output_tokens}",
238	                    path,
239	                    "#14",
240	                )
241	            )
242	
243	    def _check_system_tps_derivable(self, s: RunSummary, path: Path | None) -> None:
244	        """§9.1: system_tps must equal total_output_tokens / elapsed_duration_seconds.
245	
246	        If the result log also stores a system_tps field, verify it matches the
247	        derived value within _TPS_TOLERANCE. Always emits ok when consistent.
248	        """
249	        derived = s.system_tps
250	        stored = (s.model_extra or {}).get("system_tps")
251	        if stored is not None:
252	            rel_err = abs(float(stored) - derived) / max(abs(derived), 1e-9)
253	            if rel_err > _TPS_TOLERANCE:
254	                self._check_results.append(
255	                    err(
256	                        "metric-consistency-system-tps",
257	                        f"stored system_tps {stored:.3f} ≠ derived {derived:.3f}"
258	                        f" (rel err {rel_err:.1%})",
259	                        path,
260	                        "#9.1",
261	                    )
262	                )
263	                return
264	        self._check_results.append(
265	            ok(
266	                "metric-consistency-system-tps",
267	                f"system_tps={derived:.3f} tok/s"
268	                f" ({s.total_output_tokens} tokens / {s.elapsed_duration_seconds:.1f}s)",
269	                path,
270	                "#9.1",
271	            )
272	        )
273	
274	    def _check_tps_per_user(self, s: RunSummary, concurrency: int, path: Path | None) -> None:
275	        """§9.1: tps_per_user must equal system_tps / concurrency.
276	
277	        If the result log also stores a tps_per_user field, verify it matches the
278	        derived value within _TPS_TOLERANCE. Always emits ok when consistent.
279	        """
280	        if concurrency <= 0:
281	            self._check_results.append(
282	                err(
283	                    "metric-consistency-tps-per-user",
284	                    f"concurrency={concurrency} is not positive",
285	                    path,
286	                    "#9.1",
287	                )
288	            )
289	            return
290	        derived = s.system_tps / concurrency
291	        stored = (s.model_extra or {}).get("tps_per_user")
292	        if stored is not None:
293	            rel_err = abs(float(stored) - derived) / max(abs(derived), 1e-9)
294	            if rel_err > _TPS_TOLERANCE:
295	                self._check_results.append(
296	                    err(
297	                        "metric-consistency-tps-per-user",
298	                        f"stored tps_per_user {stored:.4f} ≠ derived system_tps/concurrency"
299	                        f" {derived:.4f} (rel err {rel_err:.1%})",
300	                        path,
301	                        "#9.1",
302	                    )
303	                )
304	                return
305	        self._check_results.append(
306	            ok(
307	                "metric-consistency-tps-per-user",
308	                f"tps_per_user={derived:.4f} tok/s/user"
309	                f" (system_tps={s.system_tps:.3f} / concurrency={concurrency})",
310	                path,
311	                "#9.1",
312	            )
313	        )
314	
315	    @model_validator(mode="after")
316	    def _check_metric_consistency(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunResult:
317	        """§14 + §9.1: validate run-log accounting invariants and tps derivability."""
318	        summary_path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("summary_path")
319	        s = self.summary
320	        self._check_duration_positive(s, summary_path)
321	        self._check_sample_accounting(s, summary_path)
322	        self._check_output_tokens_nonnegative(s, summary_path)
323	        self._check_system_tps_derivable(s, summary_path)
324	        self._check_tps_per_user(s, self.config.concurrency, summary_path)
325	        return self
326
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""Run configuration model — §8.3 measurement run YAML schema and per-run checks."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from pathlib import Path
6	
7	from pydantic import (
8	    BaseModel,
9	    ConfigDict,
10	    Field,
11	    PrivateAttr,
12	    ValidationInfo,
13	    computed_field,
14	    model_validator,
15	)

17	from .regions import classify_concurrency

20	
21	class LoadPatternConfig(BaseModel):
22	    """Load pattern settings matching the endpoints tool ``LoadPattern`` schema.
23	
24	    Attributes:
25	        type: Pattern type — must be ``"concurrency"`` for submissions (§6.1).
26	        target_concurrency: Fixed concurrency level for ``ConcurrencyScheduler`` runs.
27	        target_qps: Target queries-per-second for ``constant_qps`` runs (unused in submissions).
28	    """
29	
30	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
31	
32	    type: str
33	    target_concurrency: int | None = None
34	    target_qps: float | None = None
35	
36	
37	class RuntimeConfig(BaseModel):
38	    """Runtime duration and sampling settings matching the endpoints ``RuntimeConfig`` schema.
39	
40	    Attributes:
41	        min_duration_ms: Minimum steady-state duration in milliseconds (§6.2).
42	        max_duration_ms: Maximum duration cap (0 = unlimited).
43	        n_samples_to_issue: Fixed sample count override (``None`` = duration-based).
44	        scheduler_random_seed: RNG seed for the concurrency scheduler.
45	        dataloader_random_seed: RNG seed for dataset loading.
46	    """
47	
48	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
49	
50	    min_duration_ms: int = 600_000
51	    max_duration_ms: int = 0
52	    n_samples_to_issue: int | None = None
53	    scheduler_random_seed: int = 42
54	    dataloader_random_seed: int = 42
55	
56	
57	class ClientConfig(BaseModel):
58	    """Client-side settings matching the endpoints ``ClientConfig`` schema.
59	
60	    Attributes:
61	        stream_all_chunks: Must be ``True`` for all submission performance runs (§6.5).
62	    """
63	
64	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
65	
66	    stream_all_chunks: bool = True
67	
68	
69	class SettingsConfig(BaseModel):
70	    """Top-level ``settings`` block from the endpoints ``BenchmarkConfig`` schema.
71	
72	    Attributes:
73	        load_pattern: Load generation strategy.
74	        runtime: Duration and sampling constraints.
75	        client: Client-side streaming settings.
76	    """
77	
78	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
79	
80	    load_pattern: LoadPatternConfig
81	    runtime: RuntimeConfig = Field(default_factory=RuntimeConfig)
82	    client: ClientConfig = Field(default_factory=ClientConfig)
83	
84	
85	class RunConfig(BaseModel):
86	    """Parsed contents of ``runs/run_<N>.yaml`` (§8.3).
87	
88	    Follows the endpoints tool ``BenchmarkConfig`` schema.  The concurrency level
89	    and dataset name are surfaced as computed properties for use by checks.
90	
91	    Attributes:
92	        type: Config type — ``"submission"`` or ``"development"``.
93	        settings: Nested load-pattern, runtime, and client settings.
94	        datasets: List of dataset descriptors (``[{"name": "..."}]``).
95	    """
96	
97	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
98	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
99	
100	    type: str = "submission"
101	    settings: SettingsConfig
102	    datasets: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
103	
104	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
105	    @property
106	    def concurrency(self) -> int:
107	        """Target concurrency level derived from ``settings.load_pattern.target_concurrency``."""
108	        return self.settings.load_pattern.target_concurrency or 0
109	
110	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
111	    @property
112	    def dataset(self) -> str:
113	        """Dataset name from the first entry in ``datasets``."""
114	        if self.datasets:
115	            return self.datasets[0].get("name", "")
116	        return ""
117	
118	    @model_validator(mode="after")
119	    def _check_load_pattern(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunConfig:
120	        """§10: load_pattern.type must be 'concurrency' with target_concurrency set."""
121	        path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("yaml_path")
122	        lp = self.settings.load_pattern
123	        if lp.type != "concurrency":
124	            self._check_results.append(
125	                err(
126	                    "load-pattern",
127	                    f"Run {self.concurrency}: load_pattern.type '{lp.type}' ≠ 'concurrency'",
128	                    path,
129	                    "#10",
130	                )
131	            )
132	        elif lp.target_concurrency is None:
133	            self._check_results.append(
134	                err(
135	                    "load-pattern",
136	                    f"Run {self.concurrency}: load_pattern.type is 'concurrency'"
137	                    " but target_concurrency is not set",
138	                    path,
139	                    "#10",
140	                )
141	            )
142	        else:
143	            self._check_results.append(
144	                ok(
145	                    "load-pattern",
146	                    f"Run {self.concurrency}: load pattern OK"
147	                    f" (type=concurrency, target={lp.target_concurrency})",
148	                    path,
149	                    "#10",
150	                )
151	            )
152	        return self
153	
154	    @model_validator(mode="after")
155	    def _check_streaming(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunConfig:
156	        """§13: stream_all_chunks must be True for all submission runs."""
157	        path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("yaml_path")
158	        if not self.settings.client.stream_all_chunks:
159	            self._check_results.append(
160	                err(
161	                    "streaming-config",
162	                    f"Run {self.concurrency}: stream_all_chunks must be True",
163	                    path,
164	                    "#13",
165	                )
166	            )
167	        else:
168	            self._check_results.append(
169	                ok(
170	                    "streaming-config",
171	                    f"Run {self.concurrency}: stream_all_chunks=True",
172	                    path,
173	                    "#13",
174	                )
175	            )
176	        return self
177	
178	    @model_validator(mode="after")
179	    def _check_concurrency_range(self, info: ValidationInfo) -> RunConfig:
180	        """§9: concurrency must not exceed the high_throughput upper bound (incl. 10% margin)."""
181	        path: Path | None = (info.context or {}).get("yaml_path")
182	        regions = (info.context or {}).get("regions")
183	        if regions is None:
184	            return self
185	        actual_region = classify_concurrency(self.concurrency, regions)
186	        if actual_region is None:
187	            self._check_results.append(
188	                err(
189	                    "concurrency-in-range",
190	                    f"Concurrency {self.concurrency} exceeds max valid range"
191	                    f" (max including 10% margin: {regions.high_throughput.end})",
192	                    path,
193	                    "#9",
194	                )
195	            )
196	        else:
197	            self._check_results.append(
198	                ok(
199	                    "concurrency-in-range",
200	                    f"Concurrency {self.concurrency} valid ({actual_region})",
201	                    path,
202	                    "#9",
203	                )
204	            )
205	        return self
206
```

```text
1	"""System description model — §8.2 hardware and software metadata."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from enum import Enum
6	from typing import Literal
7	
8	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, model_validator
9	
10	
11	class Division(str, Enum):
12	    """Submission division (§2)."""
13	
14	    STANDARDIZED = "Standardized"
15	    SERVICED = "Serviced"
16	    RDI = "RDI"
17	
18	
19	class PublicationStatus(str, Enum):
20	    """Publication status category (§7)."""
21	
22	    AVAILABLE = "Available"
23	    PREVIEW = "Preview"
24	    RDI = "RDI"
25	
26	
27	class SystemDescription(BaseModel):
28	    """Parsed contents of ``systems/<system_desc_id>.json`` (§8.2).
29	
30	    Combines Endpoints-specific fields with the standard hardware metadata
31	    required by the general MLPerf submission rules (§5.7 / system_desc_id).
32	
33	    Attributes:
34	        division: Submission division (Endpoints-specific).
35	        publication_status: Publication status category (Endpoints-specific).
36	        benchmark_model: Benchmark model name matching the supported model list.
37	        max_supported_concurrency: Declared maximum concurrency ``M`` (must be > 32).
38	        endpoint_url: URL or description of the endpoint under test.
39	        serving_framework: Inference serving framework and version.
40	        submitter: Organisation submitting these results.
41	        system_name: Human-readable name for the system under test.
42	        system_type: Deployment class — ``"datacenter"`` or ``"edge"``.
43	        system_type_detail: Free-form clarification of system type.
44	        number_of_nodes: Node count in the system.
45	        host_processors_per_node: Number of host CPU sockets per node.
46	        host_processor_model_name: CPU model name.
47	        host_processor_core_count: Physical core count per CPU (provide this
48	            or ``host_processor_vcpu_count``, or both).
49	        host_processor_vcpu_count: vCPU count per CPU (virtual/cloud systems).
50	        host_memory_capacity: Total host DRAM capacity (e.g. ``"768 GB"``).
51	        host_storage_type: Primary storage device type.
52	        host_storage_capacity: Total usable storage capacity.
53	        host_networking: Network technology used (e.g. ``"InfiniBand HDR"``).
54	        host_networking_topology: Network topology description.
55	        accelerators_per_node: Number of accelerator devices per node.
56	        accelerator_model_name: Accelerator model name.
57	        accelerator_memory_capacity: HBM or device memory capacity.
58	        operating_system: OS name and version string.
59	        host_processor_frequency: CPU frequency (optional).
60	        host_processor_caches: CPU cache hierarchy description (optional).
61	        host_processor_interconnect: CPU-to-CPU interconnect (optional).
62	        accelerator_frequency: Accelerator clock frequency (optional).
63	        accelerator_on_chip_memories: On-chip SRAM/cache description (optional).
64	        accelerator_host_interconnect: Host-to-accelerator bus (optional).
65	        accelerator_memory_configuration: HBM configuration details (optional).
66	        accelerator_interconnect: Accelerator-to-accelerator interconnect (optional).
67	        accelerator_interconnect_topology: Interconnect topology (optional).
68	        host_memory_configuration: DRAM configuration details (optional).
69	        host_network_card_count: NIC description / count (optional).
70	        cooling: Cooling method (optional).
71	        other_software_stack: Additional software stack notes (optional).
72	        hw_notes: Free-form hardware notes (optional).
73	        sw_notes: Free-form software notes (optional).
74	    """
75	
76	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
77	
78	    # Endpoints-specific required fields
79	    division: Division
80	    publication_status: PublicationStatus
81	    benchmark_model: str
82	    max_supported_concurrency: int = Field(gt=32)  # M > 32 per §5.4
83	    endpoint_url: str
84	    serving_framework: str
85	
86	    # General MLPerf submission rules — required hardware fields
87	    submitter: str
88	    system_name: str
89	    system_type: Literal["datacenter", "edge"]
90	    system_type_detail: str
91	    number_of_nodes: int = Field(gt=0)
92	    host_processors_per_node: int = Field(gt=0)
93	    host_processor_model_name: str
94	    host_processor_core_count: int | None = None
95	    host_processor_vcpu_count: int | None = None
96	    host_memory_capacity: str
97	    host_storage_type: str
98	    host_storage_capacity: str
99	    host_networking: str
100	    host_networking_topology: str
101	    accelerators_per_node: int = Field(ge=0)
102	    accelerator_model_name: str
103	    accelerator_memory_capacity: str
104	    operating_system: str
105	
106	    # General MLPerf submission rules — optional hardware fields
107	    host_processor_frequency: str = ""
108	    host_processor_caches: str = ""
109	    host_processor_interconnect: str = ""
110	    accelerator_frequency: str = ""
111	    accelerator_on_chip_memories: str = ""
112	    accelerator_host_interconnect: str = ""
113	    accelerator_memory_configuration: str = ""
114	    accelerator_interconnect: str = ""
115	    accelerator_interconnect_topology: str = ""
116	    host_memory_configuration: str = ""
117	    host_network_card_count: str = ""
118	    cooling: str = ""
119	    other_software_stack: str = ""
120	    hw_notes: str = ""
121	    sw_notes: str = ""
122	
123	    @model_validator(mode="after")
124	    def _require_core_or_vcpu(self) -> SystemDescription:
125	        """At least one of host_processor_core_count or host_processor_vcpu_count must be set."""
126	        if self.host_processor_core_count is None and self.host_processor_vcpu_count is None:
127	            raise ValueError(

129	                " or host_processor_vcpu_count must be provided"
```

```text
1	"""Region boundary computation — §5 Pareto Collection Methodology.
2	
3	Implements the reference algorithm from §5.5 verbatim, using Python's built-in
4	``round()`` (banker's / round-half-to-even) for all boundary calculations.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import math
10	from dataclasses import dataclass
11	
12	
13	@dataclass(frozen=True)
14	class RegionBounds:
15	    """Inclusive ``[start, end]`` concurrency range for one region.
16	
17	    Attributes:
18	        start: Lowest valid concurrency level (inclusive).
19	        end: Highest valid concurrency level (inclusive).
20	    """
21	
22	    start: int
23	    end: int
24	
25	    def contains(self, concurrency: int) -> bool:
26	        """Return True if *concurrency* falls within this region."""
27	        return self.start <= concurrency <= self.end
28	
29	    def __str__(self) -> str:
30	        return f"{self.start}–{self.end}"
31	
32	
33	@dataclass(frozen=True)
34	class Regions:
35	    """All region boundaries for a given Maximum Supported Concurrency.
36	
37	    Attributes:
38	        low_latency: Fixed 1–32 range.
39	        low_throughput: First logarithmic throughput region.
40	        med_throughput: Second logarithmic throughput region.
41	        high_throughput: Third logarithmic throughput region, extended by the
42	            §5.4 10 % margin — spans from ``med_throughput.end + 1`` to
43	            ``ceil(M * 1.10)``.
44	    """
45	
46	    low_latency: RegionBounds
47	    low_throughput: RegionBounds
48	    med_throughput: RegionBounds
49	    high_throughput: RegionBounds
50	
51	
52	# Minimum steady-state duration per region (§6.2, illustrative — WIP).
53	MIN_DURATION_MS: dict[str, int] = {
54	    "low_latency": 600_000,
55	    "low_throughput": 1_200_000,
56	    "med_throughput": 1_200_000,
57	    "high_throughput": 1_200_000,
58	}
59	
60	
61	def compute_regions(M: int) -> Regions:
62	    """Compute region boundaries for a declared Maximum Supported Concurrency *M*.
63	
64	    This is the reference algorithm from §5.5, using banker's rounding
65	    (Python's built-in ``round()``).
66	
67	    Args:
68	        M: Maximum Supported Concurrency declared in the system description.
69	           Must be greater than 32.
70	
71	    Returns:
72	        A :class:`Regions` instance with inclusive ``[start, end]`` boundaries.
73	
74	    Raises:
75	        ValueError: If *M* is not greater than 32.
76	
77	    Example::
78	
79	        regions = compute_regions(1024)
80	        print(regions.low_throughput)  # 33–42
81	    """
82	    if M <= 32:
83	        raise ValueError(f"Maximum Supported Concurrency must be > 32, got {M}")
84	
85	    interval = math.log2(M - 32) / 3
86	
87	    low_tput_end = round(32 + 2**interval)
88	    med_tput_end = round(32 + 2 ** (2 * interval))
89	    ht_end = math.ceil(M * 1.10)  # includes §5.4 10% margin
90	
91	    return Regions(
92	        low_latency=RegionBounds(1, 32),
93	        low_throughput=RegionBounds(33, low_tput_end),
94	        med_throughput=RegionBounds(low_tput_end + 1, med_tput_end),
95	        high_throughput=RegionBounds(med_tput_end + 1, ht_end),
96	    )
97	
98	
99	def classify_concurrency(concurrency: int, regions: Regions) -> str | None:
100	    """Return the canonical region name for *concurrency*, or ``None`` if out of range.
101	
102	    The High Throughput region already includes the §5.4 10 % margin, so
103	    concurrencies up to ``ceil(M * 1.10)`` classify as ``"high_throughput"``.
104	
105	    Args:
106	        concurrency: The concurrency level to classify.
107	        regions: Pre-computed region boundaries from :func:`compute_regions`.
108	
109	    Returns:
110	        One of ``"low_latency"``, ``"low_throughput"``, ``"med_throughput"``,
111	        ``"high_throughput"``, or ``None`` if the concurrency exceeds the
112	        high throughput region (including the 10% margin).  A ``None`` return
113	        means the concurrency is out of range for submission purposes and the
114	        corresponding run should be flagged as invalid by the caller.
115	    """
116	    if regions.low_latency.contains(concurrency):
117	        return "low_latency"
118	    if regions.low_throughput.contains(concurrency):
119	        return "low_throughput"
120	    if regions.med_throughput.contains(concurrency):
121	        return "med_throughput"
122	    if regions.high_throughput.contains(concurrency):
123	        return "high_throughput"
124	    return None
125
```
</details></tool-use>
