```text
1	"""Submission checker — orchestrates §9.1 automated compliance checks.
2	
3	Loading and structural validation live here; all rule logic lives in Pydantic
4	model validators on RunConfig, RunResult, and ModelContext.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
10	
11	from .loader import (
12	    load_accuracy_result,
13	    load_result_summary,
14	    load_run_config,
15	    load_system_description,
16	)
17	from .models import (
18	    CheckResult,
19	    ModelContext,
20	    Regions,
21	    Report,
22	    RunConfig,
23	    RunResult,
24	    RunSummary,
25	    Severity,
26	    SystemDescription,
27	    compute_regions,
28	)
29	from .models import err as _err
30	from .models import ok as _ok
31	from .models import warn as _warn
32	from .structure import ModelDir, SrcDir, SubmissionDir, SystemPareto
33	
34	if TYPE_CHECKING:
35	    from pathlib import Path
36	
37	
38	class SubmissionChecker:
39	    """Validates an MLPerf Endpoints submission directory against §9.1 rules.
40	
41	    The *submission_path* should be the submitting organisation's root directory,
42	    which must contain ``systems/`` and ``pareto/`` subdirectories as specified
43	    in §8.1.
44	
45	    Args:
46	        submission_path: Root directory of the submission to validate.
47	
48	    Example::
49	
50	        checker = SubmissionChecker(Path("/submissions/acme_corp"))
51	        report = checker.run()
52	        for err in report.errors:
53	            print(err.rule, err.message)
54	    """
55	
56	    def __init__(self, submission_path: Path) -> None:
57	        self.submission_path = submission_path
58	
59	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
60	    # Public entry point
61	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	    def run(self) -> Report:
64	        """Run all §9.1 automated checks and return an aggregated
65	        :class:`~submission_checker.models.Report`.
66	
67	        Returns:
68	            A :class:`~submission_checker.models.Report` with every
69	            :class:`~submission_checker.models.CheckResult` produced.
70	        """
71	        report = Report(submission_path=self.submission_path)
72	
73	        if not self.submission_path.exists():
74	            report.results.append(
75	                _err(
76	                    "path-exists",
77	                    f"Submission path does not exist: {self.submission_path}",
78	                    self.submission_path,
79	                    "#1",
80	                )
81	            )
82	            return report
83	        report.results.append(
84	            _ok("path-exists", "Submission path exists", self.submission_path, "#1")
85	        )
86	
87	        submission_dir = SubmissionDir(root=self.submission_path)
88	        report.results.extend(submission_dir._check_results)
89	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in submission_dir._check_results):
90	            return report
91	
92	        systems_dir = submission_dir.systems_dir
93	        pareto_dir = submission_dir.pareto_dir
94	
95	        system_jsons = sorted(systems_dir.glob("*.json"))
96	        if not system_jsons:
97	            report.results.append(
98	                _err(
99	                    "system-description-present",
100	                    "No *.json files found in systems/",
101	                    systems_dir,
102	                    "#1",
103	                )
104	            )
105	            return report
106	
107	        for system_json in system_jsons:
108	            report.results.extend(self._check_system(system_json, pareto_dir))
109	
110	        return report
111	
112	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
113	    # Per-system orchestration
114	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
115	
116	    def _check_system(self, system_json: Path, pareto_dir: Path) -> list[CheckResult]:
117	        results: list[CheckResult] = []
118	        system_id = system_json.stem
119	
120	        system_desc, load_err = load_system_description(system_json)
121	        if load_err:
122	            results.append(_err("system-description-valid", load_err, system_json, "#1"))
123	            return results
124	        assert system_desc is not None
125	        results.append(
126	            _ok(
127	                "system-description-valid",
128	                f"System description valid: {system_id}",
129	                system_json,
130	                "#1",
131	            )
132	        )
133	
134	        M = system_desc.max_supported_concurrency
135	        results.append(
136	            _ok("max-concurrency-declared", f"max_supported_concurrency = {M}", system_json, "#7")
137	        )
138	
139	        try:
140	            regions = compute_regions(M)
141	        except ValueError as exc:
142	            results.append(_err("region-computation", str(exc), system_json, "#7"))
143	            return results
144	
145	        src = SrcDir(root=self.submission_path, division=system_desc.division)
146	        results.extend(src._check_results)
147	
148	        system_pareto = SystemPareto(pareto_dir=pareto_dir, system_id=system_id)
149	        results.extend(system_pareto._check_results)
150	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in system_pareto._check_results):
151	            return results
152	        system_pareto_dir = system_pareto.system_dir
153	        model_dirs = [d for d in sorted(system_pareto_dir.iterdir()) if d.is_dir()]
154	        if not model_dirs:
155	            results.append(
156	                _err(
157	                    "benchmark-model-dir",
158	                    f"No benchmark-model directories in pareto/{system_id}/",
159	                    system_pareto_dir,
160	                    "#1",
161	                )
162	            )
163	            return results
164	
165	        for model_dir in model_dirs:
166	            results.extend(self._check_model(system_id, system_desc, regions, model_dir))
167	
168	        return results
169	
170	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
171	    # Per benchmark-model orchestration
172	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
173	
174	    def _check_model(
175	        self,
176	        system_id: str,
177	        system_desc: SystemDescription,
178	        regions: Regions,
179	        model_dir: Path,
180	    ) -> list[CheckResult]:
181	        results: list[CheckResult] = []
182	        benchmark_model = model_dir.name
183	
184	        model_structure = ModelDir(
185	            root=model_dir, system_id=system_id, benchmark_model=benchmark_model
186	        )
187	        results.extend(model_structure._check_results)
188	        if any(r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in model_structure._check_results):
189	            return results
190	
191	        runs_dir = model_structure.runs_dir
192	        results_dir = model_structure.results_dir
193	        accuracy_dir = model_structure.accuracy_dir
194	
195	        run_yamls = sorted(runs_dir.glob("run_*.yaml"))
196	        if not run_yamls:
197	            results.append(
198	                _err(
199	                    "measurement-runs-present",
200	                    f"No run_*.yaml files in {runs_dir.relative_to(self.submission_path)}",
201	                    runs_dir,
202	                    "#1",
203	                )
204	            )
205	            return results
206	
207	        valid_runs: list[tuple[Path, RunConfig]] = []
208	        loaded_results: list[tuple[RunConfig, RunSummary]] = []
209	
210	        for yaml_path in run_yamls:
211	            config, config_results = load_run_config(
212	                yaml_path, context={"regions": regions, "yaml_path": yaml_path}
213	            )
214	            results.extend(config_results)
215	            if config is None:
216	                continue
217	
218	            # filename-concurrency consistency warning
219	            try:
220	                fname_concurrency = int(yaml_path.stem.split("_")[1])
221	                if fname_concurrency != config.concurrency:
222	                    results.append(
223	                        _warn(
224	                            "run-filename-concurrency",
225	                            f"{yaml_path.name}: filename concurrency {fname_concurrency}"
226	                            f" ≠ declared {config.concurrency}",
227	                            yaml_path,
228	                            "#1",
229	                        )
230	                    )
231	            except (IndexError, ValueError):
232	                pass
233	
234	            valid_runs.append((yaml_path, config))
235	
236	            summary_path = (
237	                results_dir / f"run_{config.concurrency}" / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json"
238	            )
239	            if not summary_path.exists():
240	                results.append(
241	                    _err(
242	                        "result-file-present",
243	                        f"Missing result log for run_{config.concurrency}:"
244	                        f" {summary_path.relative_to(self.submission_path)}",
245	                        summary_path,
246	                        "#1",
247	                    )
248	                )
249	                continue
250	
251	            summary, load_err = load_result_summary(summary_path)
252	            if load_err:
253	                results.append(_err("result-file-valid", load_err, summary_path, "#1"))
254	                continue
255	
256	            # RunResult validates run-duration and metric-consistency
257	            run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
258	                {"config": config, "summary": summary, "yaml_path": yaml_path},
259	                context={"regions": regions, "summary_path": summary_path},
260	            )
261	            results.extend(run_result._check_results)
262	            loaded_results.append((config, summary))
263	
264	        # Load accuracy
265	        accuracy_result = None
266	        txt_path = accuracy_dir / "accuracy.txt"
267	        if not txt_path.exists():
268	            results.append(_err("accuracy-file", "Missing accuracy/accuracy.txt", txt_path, "#15"))
269	        json_path = accuracy_dir / "accuracy_result.json"
270	        if not json_path.exists():
271	            results.append(
272	                _err("accuracy-file", "Missing accuracy/accuracy_result.json", json_path, "#15")
273	            )
274	        else:
275	            accuracy_result, acc_err = load_accuracy_result(json_path)
276	            if acc_err:
277	                results.append(_err("accuracy-valid", acc_err, json_path, "#15"))
278	                accuracy_result = None
279	
280	        # ModelContext validates run-count, regional-coverage, config-consistency, accuracy-gate
281	        model_ctx = ModelContext(
282	            system_id=system_id,
283	            system_desc=system_desc,
284	            model_dir=model_dir,
285	            regions=regions,
286	            runs_dir=runs_dir,
287	            accuracy_dir=accuracy_dir,
288	            all_run_count=len(run_yamls),
289	            valid_runs=valid_runs,
290	            loaded_results=loaded_results,
291	            accuracy_result=accuracy_result,
292	        )
293	        results.extend(model_ctx._check_results)
294	
295	        return results
296
```

```text
1	"""Utilities for loading and parsing submission artifact files."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	from pathlib import Path
7	from typing import TypeVar
8	
9	import yaml
10	from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError
11	
12	from .models import (
13	    AccuracyResult,
14	    CheckResult,
15	    RunConfig,
16	    RunSummary,
17	    Severity,
18	    SystemDescription,
19	)
20	
21	_T = TypeVar("_T", bound=BaseModel)
22	
23	
24	def _load_json(path: Path) -> tuple[dict | None, str | None]:
25	    try:
26	        return json.loads(path.read_text()), None
27	    except FileNotFoundError:
28	        return None, f"File not found: {path}"
29	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
30	        return None, f"JSON parse error in {path.name}: {exc}"
31	    except OSError as exc:
32	        return None, f"IO error reading {path.name}: {exc}"
33	
34	
35	def _load_yaml(path: Path) -> tuple[dict | None, str | None]:
36	    try:
37	        data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
38	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
39	            return None, f"Expected a YAML mapping in {path.name}"
40	        return data, None
41	    except FileNotFoundError:
42	        return None, f"File not found: {path}"
43	    except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
44	        return None, f"YAML parse error in {path.name}: {exc}"
45	    except OSError as exc:
46	        return None, f"IO error reading {path.name}: {exc}"
47	
48	
49	def _load_validated(data: dict, model_cls: type[_T], filename: str) -> tuple[_T | None, str | None]:
50	    try:
51	        return model_cls.model_validate(data), None
52	    except ValidationError as exc:
53	        first = exc.errors()[0]
54	        return None, f"Validation error in {filename}: {first['loc']} — {first['msg']}"
55	
56	
57	def load_system_description(path: Path) -> tuple[SystemDescription | None, str | None]:
58	    """Load and validate ``system_desc_id.json``.
59	
60	    Returns:
61	        A ``(model, error)`` pair — exactly one of the two is ``None``.
62	    """
63	    data, err = _load_json(path)
64	    return _load_validated(data, SystemDescription, path.name) if data is not None else (None, err)
65	
66	
67	def load_run_config(
68	    path: Path, context: dict | None = None
69	) -> tuple[RunConfig | None, list[CheckResult]]:
70	    """Load and validate a ``run_<N>.yaml`` measurement-run config.
71	
72	    Returns:
73	        A ``(model, check_results)`` pair.  On success the model is not None and
74	        check_results contains the validator-produced CheckResult entries.
75	        On failure the model is None and check_results contains a single ERROR entry.
76	    """
77	    data, load_err = _load_yaml(path)
78	    if load_err:
79	        return None, [
80	            CheckResult(
81	                rule="run-config-valid", message=load_err, severity=Severity.ERROR, path=path
82	            )
83	        ]
84	    try:
85	        instance = RunConfig.model_validate(data, context=context or {})
86	        return instance, list(instance._check_results)
87	    except ValidationError as exc:
88	        first = exc.errors()[0]
89	        return None, [
90	            CheckResult(
91	                rule="run-config-valid",
92	                message=f"Validation error in {path.name}: {first['loc']} — {first['msg']}",
93	                severity=Severity.ERROR,
94	                path=path,
95	            )
96	        ]
97	
98	
99	def load_result_summary(path: Path) -> tuple[RunSummary | None, str | None]:
100	    """Load and validate ``mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json``.
101	
102	    Returns:
103	        A ``(model, error)`` pair — exactly one of the two is ``None``.
104	    """
105	    data, err = _load_json(path)
106	    return _load_validated(data, RunSummary, path.name) if data is not None else (None, err)
107	
108	
109	def load_accuracy_result(path: Path) -> tuple[AccuracyResult | None, str | None]:
110	    """Load and validate ``accuracy_result.json``.
111	
112	    Returns:
113	        A ``(model, error)`` pair — exactly one of the two is ``None``.
114	    """
115	    data, err = _load_json(path)
116	    return _load_validated(data, AccuracyResult, path.name) if data is not None else (None, err)
117
```

```text
1	"""Tests for SubmissionChecker using pre-built fixtures from test_submissions/."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	from pathlib import Path
7	from unittest.mock import patch

11	from submission_checker.checker import SubmissionChecker
12	from submission_checker.models import CheckResult, Report, Severity

14	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	# Helpers
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	
18	
19	def _errors(report: Report, rule: str) -> list[CheckResult]:
20	    return [r for r in report.results if r.rule == rule and r.severity == Severity.ERROR]
21	
22	
23	def _warnings(report: Report, rule: str) -> list[CheckResult]:
24	    return [r for r in report.results if r.rule == rule and r.severity == Severity.WARNING]
25	
26	
27	def _check(path: Path) -> Report:
28	    return SubmissionChecker(path).run()
29

36	class TestValidStandardized:

38	        assert _check(valid_standardized).passed
39	
40	    def test_all_regions_covered(self, valid_standardized):
41	        report = _check(valid_standardized)
42	        for rule in [
43	            "low-latency-coverage",
44	            "low-throughput-coverage",
45	            "med-throughput-coverage",
46	            "high-throughput-coverage",
47	        ]:
48	            assert not _errors(report, rule), f"{rule} should pass"
49	
50	    def test_metric_consistency(self, valid_standardized):
51	        report = _check(valid_standardized)
52	        assert not _errors(report, "metric-consistency-duration")
53	        assert not _errors(report, "metric-consistency-accounting")

261	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
262	
263	_SYSTEM_DESC = {
264	    "division": "Serviced",
265	    "publication_status": "Available",
266	    "benchmark_model": "llama3-70b",
267	    "max_supported_concurrency": 1024,
268	    "endpoint_url": "http://localhost",
269	    "serving_framework": "vLLM",
270	    "submitter": "Test Org",
271	    "system_name": "test-sys",
272	    "system_type": "datacenter",
273	    "system_type_detail": "",
274	    "number_of_nodes": 1,
275	    "host_processors_per_node": 2,
276	    "host_processor_model_name": "AMD EPYC",
277	    "host_processor_core_count": 64,
278	    "host_memory_capacity": "512 GB",
279	    "host_storage_type": "NVMe",
280	    "host_storage_capacity": "10 TB",
281	    "host_networking": "InfiniBand",
282	    "host_networking_topology": "Single switch",
283	    "accelerators_per_node": 8,
284	    "accelerator_model_name": "H100",
285	    "accelerator_memory_capacity": "80 GB",
286	    "operating_system": "Ubuntu 22.04",
287	}
288	
289	_SUMMARY = {
290	    "n_samples_issued": 1000,
291	    "n_samples_completed": 1000,
292	    "n_samples_failed": 0,
293	    "duration_ns": 1_200_000_000_000.0,
294	    "ttft": {"total": 0.0, "percentiles": {"50": 150_000_000.0, "95": 300_000_000.0}},
295	    "output_sequence_lengths": {"total": 500_000.0, "percentiles": {}},
296	}
297	
298	_ACCURACY = {"metric": "rouge1", "score": 0.45, "quality_target": 0.43, "passed": True}
299	
300	# Concurrencies that cover all four regions for M=1024
301	# LT: 33–42 → 38; MT: 43–175 → 88; HT: 176–1126 → 256, 512, 768, 1000
302	_CONCURRENCIES = [16, 38, 88, 256, 512, 768, 1000]
303	
304	
305	def _make_run_yaml(concurrency: int) -> dict:
306	    return {
307	        "type": "submission",
308	        "settings": {
309	            "load_pattern": {"type": "concurrency", "target_concurrency": concurrency},
310	            "runtime": {"min_duration_ms": 1_200_000},
311	            "client": {"stream_all_chunks": True},
312	        },
313	        "datasets": [{"name": "llm-perf-dataset-v1"}],
314	    }
315	
316	
317	def _build_submission(
318	    root: Path,
319	    system_id: str = "test-sys",
320	    system_desc: dict | None = None,
321	    concurrencies: list[int] | None = None,
322	    write_runs: bool = True,
323	    write_results: bool = True,
324	    write_accuracy: bool = True,
325	    write_accuracy_json: bool = True,
326	    accuracy_data: dict | None = None,
327	    model: str = "llama3-70b",
328	) -> Path:
329	    """Build a minimal valid (or deliberately broken) submission directory."""
330	    desc = system_desc if system_desc is not None else _SYSTEM_DESC.copy()
331	    desc["benchmark_model"] = model
332	    concs = concurrencies if concurrencies is not None else _CONCURRENCIES
333	
334	    systems_dir = root / "systems"
335	    systems_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
336	    (systems_dir / f"{system_id}.json").write_text(json.dumps(desc))
337	
338	    pareto_dir = root / "pareto"
339	    model_dir = pareto_dir / system_id / model
340	    runs_dir = model_dir / "runs"
341	    results_dir = model_dir / "results"
342	    accuracy_dir = model_dir / "accuracy"
343	    for d in (runs_dir, results_dir, accuracy_dir):
344	        d.mkdir(parents=True)
345	
346	    if write_runs:
347	        for c in concs:
348	            (runs_dir / f"run_{c}.yaml").write_text(yaml.dump(_make_run_yaml(c)))
349	
350	    if write_results:
351	        for c in concs:
352	            result_dir = results_dir / f"run_{c}"
353	            result_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
354	            (result_dir / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SUMMARY))
355	
356	    if write_accuracy:
357	        (accuracy_dir / "accuracy.txt").write_text("ROUGE-1: 0.45")
358	    if write_accuracy_json:
359	        data = accuracy_data if accuracy_data is not None else _ACCURACY
360	        (accuracy_dir / "accuracy_result.json").write_text(json.dumps(data))
361	
362	    return root
363	
364	
365	class TestCheckerEdgeCases:
366	    """Targeted tests to cover checker.py error paths not exercised by fixture tests."""
367	
368	    def test_nonexistent_path(self, tmp_path):
369	        """path-exists error when submission_path does not exist."""
370	        report = _check(tmp_path / "does_not_exist")
371	        assert _errors(report, "path-exists")
372	
373	    def test_missing_required_dirs_early_exit(self, tmp_path):
374	        """SubmissionDir structure errors cause early return from run()."""
375	        # Only systems/ present — pareto/ missing → structure error → early exit
376	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
377	        report = _check(tmp_path)
378	        assert _errors(report, "required-dir")
379	        # Should not have processed any systems
380	        assert not any(r.rule == "system-description-present" for r in report.results)
381	
382	    def test_no_system_json_files(self, tmp_path):
383	        """system-description-present error when systems/ has no *.json files."""
384	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
385	        (tmp_path / "pareto").mkdir()
386	        report = _check(tmp_path)
387	        assert _errors(report, "system-description-present")
388	
389	    def test_invalid_system_json(self, tmp_path):
390	        """system-description-valid error when system JSON is malformed."""
391	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
392	        (tmp_path / "pareto").mkdir()
393	        (tmp_path / "systems" / "bad-sys.json").write_text("{bad json")
394	        report = _check(tmp_path)
395	        assert _errors(report, "system-description-valid")
396	
397	    def test_missing_pareto_system_dir_early_exit(self, tmp_path):
398	        """pareto-dir-exists error when pareto/<system_id>/ is absent."""
399	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
400	        (tmp_path / "pareto").mkdir()
401	        (tmp_path / "systems" / "test-sys.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SYSTEM_DESC))
402	        report = _check(tmp_path)
403	        assert _errors(report, "pareto-dir-exists")
404	
405	    def test_empty_pareto_system_dir(self, tmp_path):
406	        """benchmark-model-dir error when pareto/<system_id>/ has no subdirectories."""
407	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
408	        (tmp_path / "systems" / "test-sys.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SYSTEM_DESC))
409	        pareto_sys = tmp_path / "pareto" / "test-sys"
410	        pareto_sys.mkdir(parents=True)
411	        report = _check(tmp_path)
412	        assert _errors(report, "benchmark-model-dir")
413	
414	    def test_missing_model_subdirs_early_exit(self, tmp_path):
415	        """pareto-subdir error when runs/ or results/ or accuracy/ is absent."""
416	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
417	        (tmp_path / "systems" / "test-sys.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SYSTEM_DESC))
418	        model_dir = tmp_path / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b"
419	        # Only runs/ present — results/ and accuracy/ missing
420	        (model_dir / "runs").mkdir(parents=True)
421	        report = _check(tmp_path)
422	        assert _errors(report, "pareto-subdir")
423	        # Should not attempt to list run_*.yaml (early exit after structure errors)
424	        assert not any(r.rule == "measurement-runs-present" for r in report.results)
425	
426	    def test_no_run_yamls(self, tmp_path):
427	        """measurement-runs-present error when runs/ has no run_*.yaml files."""
428	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path, write_runs=False, write_results=False)
429	        report = _check(root)
430	        assert _errors(report, "measurement-runs-present")
431	
432	    def test_missing_result_log(self, tmp_path):
433	        """result-file-present error when results/run_<N>/ log is absent."""
434	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path, write_results=False)
435	        report = _check(root)
436	        assert _errors(report, "result-file-present")
437	
438	    def test_invalid_result_log(self, tmp_path):
439	        """result-file-valid error when the result log JSON is malformed."""
440	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path)
441	        # Overwrite one summary with invalid JSON
442	        bad_path = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "results" / "run_16"
443	        bad_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
444	        (bad_path / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json").write_text("{bad")
445	        report = _check(root)
446	        assert _errors(report, "result-file-valid")
447	
448	    def test_missing_accuracy_txt(self, tmp_path):
449	        """accuracy-file error when accuracy/accuracy.txt is absent."""
450	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path, write_accuracy=False)
451	        report = _check(root)
452	        assert _errors(report, "accuracy-file")
453	
454	    def test_missing_accuracy_json(self, tmp_path):
455	        """accuracy-file error when accuracy/accuracy_result.json is absent."""
456	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path, write_accuracy_json=False)
457	        report = _check(root)
458	        assert _errors(report, "accuracy-file")
459	
460	    def test_invalid_accuracy_json(self, tmp_path):
461	        """accuracy-valid error when accuracy_result.json is malformed."""
462	        root = _build_submission(
463	            tmp_path,
464	            accuracy_data={"metric": "rouge1"},  # missing required fields
465	        )
466	        report = _check(root)
467	        assert _errors(report, "accuracy-valid")
468	
469	    def test_run_filename_concurrency_mismatch(self, tmp_path):
470	        """run-filename-concurrency warning when filename concurrency ≠ declared concurrency."""
471	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path)
472	        # Add a run file whose name says 999 but YAML declares 64
473	        mismatch_yaml = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "runs" / "run_999.yaml"
474	        mismatch_yaml.write_text(yaml.dump(_make_run_yaml(64)))
475	        # Also add the matching result dir so it doesn't error on result-file-present
476	        result_dir = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "results" / "run_64"
477	        result_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
478	        (result_dir / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SUMMARY))
479	        report = _check(root)
480	        assert _warnings(report, "run-filename-concurrency")
481	
482	    def test_invalid_run_yaml_is_skipped(self, tmp_path):
483	        """A run_*.yaml that fails validation does not crash the checker."""
484	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path)
485	        bad_yaml = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "runs" / "run_99.yaml"
486	        bad_yaml.write_text("{bad yaml [")
487	        report = _check(root)
488	        # Should produce a run-config-valid error for the bad file
489	        assert _errors(report, "run-config-valid")
490	
491	    def test_region_computation_error(self, tmp_path):
492	        """region-computation error when compute_regions raises ValueError."""
493	        # compute_regions only raises if M <= 32, but SystemDescription enforces M > 32.
494	        # Patch compute_regions to simulate an unexpected ValueError.
495	        (tmp_path / "systems").mkdir()
496	        (tmp_path / "systems" / "test-sys.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SYSTEM_DESC))
497	        pareto_sys = tmp_path / "pareto" / "test-sys"
498	        pareto_sys.mkdir(parents=True)
499	        (pareto_sys / "llama3-70b").mkdir()
500	        with patch(
501	            "submission_checker.checker.compute_regions",
502	            side_effect=ValueError("M must be > 32"),
503	        ):
504	            report = _check(tmp_path)
505	        assert _errors(report, "region-computation")
506	
507	    def test_run_filename_non_numeric_suffix_ignored(self, tmp_path):
508	        """Filename parsing errors (non-numeric suffix) are silently ignored."""
509	        root = _build_submission(tmp_path)
510	        # run_abc.yaml — stem is "run_abc", int("abc") raises ValueError
511	        # This tests the except (IndexError, ValueError): pass branch
512	        bad_name = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "runs" / "run_abc.yaml"
513	        bad_name.write_text(yaml.dump(_make_run_yaml(64)))
514	        result_dir = root / "pareto" / "test-sys" / "llama3-70b" / "results" / "run_64"
515	        result_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
516	        (result_dir / "mlperf_endpoints_log_summary.json").write_text(json.dumps(_SUMMARY))
517	        report = _check(root)
518	        # No run-filename-concurrency warning — the ValueError was swallowed
519	        assert not _warnings(report, "run-filename-concurrency")
520
```

```text
1	"""Unit tests for check validators and structural gate checks."""

32	_HW = {
33	    "submitter": "Test Org",
34	    "system_name": "test-sys",
35	    "system_type": "datacenter",
36	    "system_type_detail": "",
37	    "number_of_nodes": 1,
38	    "host_processors_per_node": 2,
39	    "host_processor_model_name": "AMD EPYC",
40	    "host_processor_core_count": 64,
41	    "host_memory_capacity": "512 GB",
42	    "host_storage_type": "NVMe",
43	    "host_storage_capacity": "10 TB",
44	    "host_networking": "InfiniBand",
45	    "host_networking_topology": "Single switch",
46	    "accelerators_per_node": 8,
47	    "accelerator_model_name": "H100",
48	    "accelerator_memory_capacity": "80 GB",
49	    "operating_system": "Ubuntu 22.04",
50	}
51	
52	_M = 1024
53	_REGIONS = compute_regions(_M)
54	
55	
56	def _system_desc(
57	    division: Division = Division.STANDARDIZED,
58	    benchmark_model: str = "llama3-70b",
59	    **kwargs,
60	) -> SystemDescription:
61	    return SystemDescription(
62	        division=division,
63	        publication_status=PublicationStatus.AVAILABLE,
64	        benchmark_model=benchmark_model,
65	        max_supported_concurrency=_M,
66	        endpoint_url="http://localhost",
67	        serving_framework="vLLM",
68	        **_HW,
69	        **kwargs,
70	    )
71	
72	
73	def _config(concurrency: int = 64, stream: bool = True, lp_type: str = "concurrency") -> RunConfig:
74	    return RunConfig(
75	        settings=SettingsConfig(
76	            load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type=lp_type, target_concurrency=concurrency),
77	            runtime=RuntimeConfig(min_duration_ms=1_200_000),
78	            client=ClientConfig(stream_all_chunks=stream),
79	        ),
80	        datasets=[{"name": "mlperf-perf-dataset-v1"}],
81	    )
82	
83	
84	def _summary(
85	    n_completed: int = 1000,
86	    n_issued: int = 1000,
87	    n_failed: int = 0,
88	    duration_ns: float = 1_200_000_000_000.0,
89	    total_tokens: float = 500_000.0,
90	) -> RunSummary:
91	    return RunSummary(
92	        n_samples_completed=n_completed,
93	        n_samples_issued=n_issued,
94	        n_samples_failed=n_failed,
95	        duration_ns=duration_ns,
96	        ttft=PercentileStats(total=0.0, percentiles={"50": 150_000_000.0, "95": 300_000_000.0}),
97	        output_sequence_lengths=PercentileStats(total=total_tokens),
98	    )
99	
100	
101	def _passed(results: list[CheckResult]) -> bool:
102	    return all(r.severity != Severity.ERROR for r in results)
103	
104	
105	def _model_ctx(
106	    tmp_path: Path,
107	    all_run_count: int = 7,
108	    valid_runs: list[tuple[Path, RunConfig]] | None = None,
109	    loaded_results: list[tuple[RunConfig, RunSummary]] | None = None,
110	    system_desc: SystemDescription | None = None,
111	    model_name: str = "llama3-70b",
112	    accuracy_result: AccuracyResult | None = None,
113	) -> ModelContext:
114	    model_dir = tmp_path / model_name
115	    model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
116	    (model_dir / "runs").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
117	    (model_dir / "results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
118	    (model_dir / "accuracy").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
119	    return ModelContext(
120	        system_id="test-sys",
121	        system_desc=system_desc or _system_desc(benchmark_model=model_name),
122	        model_dir=model_dir,
123	        regions=_REGIONS,
124	        runs_dir=model_dir / "runs",
125	        accuracy_dir=model_dir / "accuracy",
126	        all_run_count=all_run_count,
127	        valid_runs=valid_runs or [],
128	        loaded_results=loaded_results or [],
129	        accuracy_result=accuracy_result,
130	    )
131	
132

138	class TestSubmissionDir:

141	        # pareto/ intentionally absent

150	        assert _passed(sd._check_results)

381	class TestMetricConsistencyValidator:
382	    def test_zero_duration_errors(self, tmp_path):
383	        config = _config(concurrency=64)
384	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
385	            {
386	                "config": config,
387	                "summary": _summary(duration_ns=0.0),
388	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
389	            },
390	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
391	        )
392	        assert any(

394	            for r in run_result._check_results
395	        )
396	
397	    def test_negative_duration_errors(self, tmp_path):
398	        config = _config(concurrency=64)
399	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
400	            {
401	                "config": config,
402	                "summary": _summary(duration_ns=-1.0),

404	            },

409	            for r in run_result._check_results

412	    def test_accounting_mismatch_errors(self, tmp_path):
413	        config = _config(concurrency=64)
414	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
415	            {
416	                "config": config,
417	                "summary": _summary(n_completed=990, n_issued=1000, n_failed=5),
418	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
419	            },
420	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
421	        )
422	        # completed(990) + failed(5) = 995 ≠ issued(1000)
423	        assert any(
424	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-accounting" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
425	            for r in run_result._check_results
426	        )

428	    def test_valid_summary_passes(self, tmp_path):
429	        config = _config(concurrency=64)
430	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
431	            {"config": config, "summary": _summary(), "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"},
432	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
433	        )
434	        assert all(r.severity != Severity.ERROR for r in run_result._check_results)
435	
436	    def test_negative_output_tokens_errors(self, tmp_path):
437	        """Negative output token total must produce a metric-consistency-output-tokens error."""
438	        config = _config(concurrency=64)
439	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
440	            {
441	                "config": config,
442	                "summary": _summary(total_tokens=-1.0),
443	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
444	            },
445	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
446	        )
447	        assert any(

449	            for r in run_result._check_results
450	        )
451	
452	
453	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
454	# TPS consistency checks (§9.1)
455	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
456	
457	# Derived values for the default _summary():
458	#   total_output_tokens = 500_000, duration_ns = 1_200_000_000_000 (1200 s)
459	#   system_tps = 500_000 / 1200 ≈ 416.667 tok/s
460	#   tps_per_user (concurrency=64) ≈ 6.5104 tok/s/user
461	
462	
463	def _summary_with(**extras) -> RunSummary:
464	    """Return the default summary but with extra fields stored for consistency checks."""
465	    return RunSummary(
466	        n_samples_completed=1000,
467	        n_samples_issued=1000,
468	        n_samples_failed=0,
469	        duration_ns=1_200_000_000_000.0,
470	        ttft=PercentileStats(total=0.0, percentiles={"50": 150_000_000.0, "95": 300_000_000.0}),
471	        output_sequence_lengths=PercentileStats(total=500_000.0),
472	        **extras,
473	    )
474	
475	
476	class TestTpsConsistencyValidator:
477	    def test_system_tps_derivable_ok(self, tmp_path):
478	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
479	            {"config": _config(concurrency=64), "summary": _summary(), "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"},
480	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
481	        )
482	        assert any(
483	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-system-tps" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR
484	            for r in run_result._check_results
485	        )
486	
487	    def test_system_tps_stored_match_ok(self, tmp_path):
488	        """Stored system_tps matching derived value within 1% passes."""
489	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
490	            {
491	                "config": _config(concurrency=64),
492	                "summary": _summary_with(system_tps=416.67),  # derived ≈ 416.667
493	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
494	            },
495	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
496	        )
497	        assert any(
498	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-system-tps" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR
499	            for r in run_result._check_results
500	        )
501	
502	    def test_system_tps_stored_mismatch_errors(self, tmp_path):
503	        """Stored system_tps differing from derived by >1% is an error."""
504	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
505	            {
506	                "config": _config(concurrency=64),
507	                "summary": _summary_with(system_tps=999.0),  # derived ≈ 416.667
508	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
509	            },
510	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
511	        )
512	        assert any(
513	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-system-tps" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
514	            for r in run_result._check_results
515	        )
516	
517	    def test_tps_per_user_derivable_ok(self, tmp_path):
518	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
519	            {"config": _config(concurrency=64), "summary": _summary(), "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"},
520	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
521	        )
522	        assert any(
523	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-tps-per-user" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR
524	            for r in run_result._check_results
525	        )
526	
527	    def test_tps_per_user_stored_match_ok(self, tmp_path):
528	        """Stored tps_per_user matching system_tps/concurrency within 1% passes."""
529	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
530	            {
531	                "config": _config(concurrency=64),
532	                "summary": _summary_with(tps_per_user=6.51),  # derived ≈ 6.5104
533	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
534	            },
535	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
536	        )
537	        assert any(
538	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-tps-per-user" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR
539	            for r in run_result._check_results
540	        )
541	
542	    def test_tps_per_user_stored_mismatch_errors(self, tmp_path):
543	        """Stored tps_per_user differing from system_tps/concurrency by >1% is an error."""
544	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
545	            {
546	                "config": _config(concurrency=64),
547	                "summary": _summary_with(tps_per_user=999.0),  # derived ≈ 6.5104
548	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
549	            },
550	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
551	        )
552	        assert any(
553	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-tps-per-user" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
554	            for r in run_result._check_results
555	        )
556	
557	    def test_tps_per_user_zero_concurrency_errors(self, tmp_path):
558	        """concurrency=0 (target_concurrency=None) must error rather than divide by zero."""
559	        config = RunConfig(
560	            settings=SettingsConfig(
561	                load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type="concurrency", target_concurrency=None),
562	                runtime=RuntimeConfig(min_duration_ms=1_200_000),
563	                client=ClientConfig(stream_all_chunks=True),
564	            ),
565	            datasets=[{"name": "mlperf-perf-dataset-v1"}],
566	        )
567	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
568	            {"config": config, "summary": _summary(), "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"},
569	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
570	        )
571	        assert any(
572	            r.rule == "metric-consistency-tps-per-user" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
573	            for r in run_result._check_results

577	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
578	# Minimum query count (§12)
579	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
580	
581	
582	def _config_with_dataset(dataset: str, concurrency: int = 64) -> RunConfig:
583	    return RunConfig(
584	        settings=SettingsConfig(
585	            load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type="concurrency", target_concurrency=concurrency),
586	            runtime=RuntimeConfig(min_duration_ms=1_200_000),
587	            client=ClientConfig(stream_all_chunks=True),
588	        ),
589	        datasets=[{"name": dataset}],
590	    )
591	
592	
593	class TestMinQueryCountValidator:
594	    def test_meets_minimum_ok(self, tmp_path):
595	        for dataset, min_q in MIN_QUERY_COUNT.items():
596	            run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
597	                {
598	                    "config": _config_with_dataset(dataset),
599	                    "summary": _summary(n_completed=min_q),
600	                    "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
601	                },
602	                context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
603	            )
604	            assert any(
605	                r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR
606	                for r in run_result._check_results
607	            ), f"Expected ok for dataset '{dataset}' with {min_q} completed"
608	
609	    def test_below_minimum_errors(self, tmp_path):
610	        for dataset, min_q in MIN_QUERY_COUNT.items():
611	            if min_q == 0:
612	                continue
613	            run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
614	                {
615	                    "config": _config_with_dataset(dataset),
616	                    "summary": _summary(n_completed=min_q - 1),
617	                    "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
618	                },
619	                context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
620	            )
621	            assert any(
622	                r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
623	                for r in run_result._check_results
624	            ), f"Expected error for dataset '{dataset}' with {min_q - 1} completed"
625	
626	    def test_unknown_dataset_skipped(self, tmp_path):
627	        run_result = RunResult.model_validate(
628	            {
629	                "config": _config_with_dataset("unknown-dataset-xyz"),
630	                "summary": _summary(n_completed=0),
631	                "yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml",
632	            },
633	            context={"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"},
634	        )
635	        assert not any(r.rule == "min-query-count" for r in run_result._check_results)
636	
637	    def test_dataset_a_boundary(self, tmp_path):
638	        """dataset-a requires exactly 1 query — 0 fails, 1 passes."""
639	        base = {"yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"}
640	        ctx = {"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"}
641	
642	        fail = RunResult.model_validate(
643	            {"config": _config_with_dataset("dataset-a"), "summary": _summary(n_completed=0), **base}, context=ctx
644	        )
645	        assert any(r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in fail._check_results)
646	
647	        ok_result = RunResult.model_validate(
648	            {"config": _config_with_dataset("dataset-a"), "summary": _summary(n_completed=1), **base}, context=ctx
649	        )
650	        assert any(r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR for r in ok_result._check_results)
651	
652	    def test_dataset_c_boundary(self, tmp_path):
653	        """dataset-c requires 100 queries — 99 fails, 100 passes."""
654	        base = {"yaml_path": tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"}
655	        ctx = {"summary_path": tmp_path / "summary.json"}
656	
657	        fail = RunResult.model_validate(
658	            {"config": _config_with_dataset("dataset-c"), "summary": _summary(n_completed=99), **base}, context=ctx
659	        )
660	        assert any(r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in fail._check_results)
661	
662	        ok_result = RunResult.model_validate(
663	            {"config": _config_with_dataset("dataset-c"), "summary": _summary(n_completed=100), **base}, context=ctx
664	        )
665	        assert any(r.rule == "min-query-count" and r.severity != Severity.ERROR for r in ok_result._check_results)
666

669	# ModelContext validators

671	
672	
673	class TestRunCountValidator:
674	    def test_too_few(self, tmp_path):
675	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, all_run_count=3)
676	        assert any(
677	            r.rule == "run-count" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in ctx._check_results
678	        )
679	
680	    def test_cap_exceeded(self, tmp_path):
681	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, all_run_count=33)
682	        assert any(r.rule == "run-cap" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in ctx._check_results)
683	
684	    def test_valid_count(self, tmp_path):
685	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, all_run_count=10)
686	        assert all(
687	            r.severity != Severity.ERROR
688	            for r in ctx._check_results
689	            if r.rule in ("run-count", "run-cap")
690	        )
691	
692	
693	class TestRegionalCoverageValidator:
694	    def test_no_runs_all_regions_missing(self, tmp_path):
695	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, valid_runs=[])
696	        coverage_rules = {
697	            "low-latency-coverage",
698	            "low-throughput-coverage",
699	            "med-throughput-coverage",
700	            "high-throughput-coverage",
701	        }
702	        errors = {
703	            r.rule
704	            for r in ctx._check_results
705	            if r.severity == Severity.ERROR and r.rule in coverage_rules
706	        }
707	        assert errors == coverage_rules
708	
709	    def test_concurrency_in_low_latency(self, tmp_path):
710	        yaml_path = tmp_path / "llama3-70b" / "runs" / "run_16.yaml"
711	        valid_runs = [(yaml_path, _config(concurrency=16))]
712	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, valid_runs=valid_runs)
713	        assert all(
714	            r.severity != Severity.ERROR
715	            for r in ctx._check_results
716	            if r.rule == "low-latency-coverage"
717	        )
718	
719	
720	class TestConfigConsistencyValidator:
721	    def test_inconsistent_datasets(self, tmp_path):
722	        c1 = RunConfig(
723	            settings=SettingsConfig(
724	                load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type="concurrency", target_concurrency=64),
725	                runtime=RuntimeConfig(),
726	                client=ClientConfig(),
727	            ),
728	            datasets=[{"name": "dataset-a"}],
729	        )
730	        c2 = RunConfig(
731	            settings=SettingsConfig(
732	                load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type="concurrency", target_concurrency=128),
733	                runtime=RuntimeConfig(),
734	                client=ClientConfig(),
735	            ),
736	            datasets=[{"name": "dataset-b"}],
737	        )
738	        s = _summary()
739	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, loaded_results=[(c1, s), (c2, s)])
740	        assert any(
741	            r.rule == "config-consistency-dataset" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR
742	            for r in ctx._check_results
743	        )
744	
745	    def test_model_name_mismatch(self, tmp_path):
746	        # model_dir.name will be "wrong-model", system_desc.benchmark_model is "wrong-model"
747	        # but _model_ctx uses model_name for both, so test a case where they differ
748	        model_dir = tmp_path / "actual-name"
749	        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
750	        (model_dir / "runs").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
751	        (model_dir / "results").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
752	        (model_dir / "accuracy").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
753	        s = _summary()
754	        ctx = ModelContext(
755	            system_id="test-sys",
756	            system_desc=_system_desc(benchmark_model="expected-name"),
757	            model_dir=model_dir,
758	            regions=_REGIONS,
759	            runs_dir=model_dir / "runs",
760	            accuracy_dir=model_dir / "accuracy",
761	            all_run_count=7,
762	            valid_runs=[],
763	            loaded_results=[(_config(), s)],
764	            accuracy_result=None,
765	        )
766	        assert any(
767	            r.rule == "config-consistency-model" and r.severity == Severity.WARNING
768	            for r in ctx._check_results
769	        )
770	
771	    def test_empty_results_skips(self, tmp_path):
772	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, loaded_results=[])
773	        # config-consistency-dataset should not appear when loaded_results is empty
774	        assert not any(r.rule == "config-consistency-dataset" for r in ctx._check_results)
775	
776	
777	class TestAccuracyGateValidator:
778	    def test_no_accuracy_result_skips(self, tmp_path):
779	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, accuracy_result=None)
780	        assert not any(r.rule == "accuracy-gate" for r in ctx._check_results)
781	
782	    def test_passed_accuracy_gate(self, tmp_path):
783	        ar = AccuracyResult(metric="rouge1", score=0.45, quality_target=0.43, passed=True)
784	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, accuracy_result=ar)
785	        assert any(
786	            r.rule == "accuracy-gate" and r.severity == Severity.INFO for r in ctx._check_results
787	        )
788	        assert not any(
789	            r.rule == "accuracy-gate" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in ctx._check_results
790	        )
791	
792	    def test_failed_accuracy_gate(self, tmp_path):
793	        ar = AccuracyResult(metric="rouge1", score=0.30, quality_target=0.43, passed=False)
794	        ctx = _model_ctx(tmp_path, accuracy_result=ar)
795	        assert any(
796	            r.rule == "accuracy-gate" and r.severity == Severity.ERROR for r in ctx._check_results
797	        )
```

```text
1	"""Tests for Pydantic submission models."""
2	
3	from pathlib import Path
4	
5	import pytest
6	from pydantic import ValidationError
7	
8	from submission_checker.models import (
9	    AccuracyResult,
10	    CheckResult,
11	    ClientConfig,
12	    Division,
13	    LoadPatternConfig,
14	    PercentileStats,
15	    PublicationStatus,
16	    Report,
17	    RunConfig,
18	    RunSummary,
19	    RuntimeConfig,
20	    SettingsConfig,
21	    Severity,
22	    SystemDescription,
23	)

25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# CheckResult / Report (infrastructure models)
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	
30	def test_check_result_passed_for_non_error():
31	    result = CheckResult(rule="r", message="ok", severity=Severity.WARNING)
32	    assert result.passed
33	
34	
35	def test_check_result_not_passed_for_error():
36	    result = CheckResult(rule="r", message="bad", severity=Severity.ERROR)
37	    assert not result.passed
38	
39	
40	def test_check_result_is_immutable():
41	    result = CheckResult(rule="r", message="ok")
42	    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
43	        result.rule = "changed"  # type: ignore[misc]
44	
45	
46	def test_check_result_serialises_to_dict():
47	    result = CheckResult(rule="r", message="ok", severity=Severity.INFO)
48	    data = result.model_dump()
49	    assert data["rule"] == "r"
50	    assert data["severity"] == Severity.INFO
51	    assert data["passed"] is True
52	
53	
54	def test_check_result_rejects_invalid_severity():
55	    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
56	        CheckResult(rule="r", message="x", severity="not-a-severity")  # type: ignore[arg-type]
57	
58	
59	def test_report_errors_and_warnings(tmp_path: Path):
60	    report = Report(submission_path=tmp_path)
61	    report.results = [
62	        CheckResult(rule="a", message="err", severity=Severity.ERROR),
63	        CheckResult(rule="b", message="warn", severity=Severity.WARNING),
64	        CheckResult(rule="c", message="info", severity=Severity.INFO),
65	    ]
66	    assert len(report.errors) == 1
67	    assert len(report.warnings) == 1
68	    assert not report.passed
69	
70	
71	def test_report_passed_with_no_errors(tmp_path: Path):
72	    report = Report(submission_path=tmp_path)
73	    report.results = [CheckResult(rule="b", message="warn", severity=Severity.WARNING)]
74	    assert report.passed
75	
76	
77	def test_report_model_dump_includes_computed_fields(tmp_path: Path):
78	    report = Report(
79	        submission_path=tmp_path,
80	        results=[CheckResult(rule="r", message="bad", severity=Severity.ERROR)],
81	    )
82	    data = report.model_dump()
83	    assert data["passed"] is False
84	    assert len(data["errors"]) == 1

90	
91	_HW_FIELDS = {
92	    "submitter": "Test Org",
93	    "system_name": "test-node",
94	    "system_type": "datacenter",
95	    "system_type_detail": "",
96	    "number_of_nodes": 1,
97	    "host_processors_per_node": 2,
98	    "host_processor_model_name": "Intel Xeon Gold 6148",
99	    "host_processor_core_count": 20,
100	    "host_memory_capacity": "384 GB",
101	    "host_storage_type": "NVMe SSD",
102	    "host_storage_capacity": "10 TB",
103	    "host_networking": "InfiniBand EDR",
104	    "host_networking_topology": "Single switch",
105	    "accelerators_per_node": 8,
106	    "accelerator_model_name": "NVIDIA A100-SXM4-80GB",
107	    "accelerator_memory_capacity": "80 GB HBM2e",
108	    "operating_system": "Ubuntu 20.04",
109	}
110	
111	
112	def test_system_description_valid():
113	    sd = SystemDescription(
114	        division=Division.STANDARDIZED,
115	        publication_status=PublicationStatus.AVAILABLE,
116	        benchmark_model="llama3-70b",
117	        max_supported_concurrency=1024,
118	        endpoint_url="https://example.com",
119	        serving_framework="vLLM 0.4.0",
120	        **_HW_FIELDS,
121	    )
122	    assert sd.division == Division.STANDARDIZED
123	    assert sd.max_supported_concurrency == 1024
124	
125	
126	def test_system_description_rejects_m_le_32():
127	    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
128	        SystemDescription(
129	            division=Division.STANDARDIZED,
130	            publication_status=PublicationStatus.AVAILABLE,
131	            benchmark_model="llama3-70b",
132	            max_supported_concurrency=32,  # must be > 32
133	            endpoint_url="https://example.com",
134	            serving_framework="vLLM 0.4.0",
135	            **_HW_FIELDS,
136	        )
137	
138	
139	def test_system_description_rejects_missing_core_and_vcpu():
140	    hw = {k: v for k, v in _HW_FIELDS.items() if k not in ("host_processor_core_count",)}
141	    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
142	        SystemDescription(
143	            division=Division.STANDARDIZED,
144	            publication_status=PublicationStatus.AVAILABLE,
145	            benchmark_model="llama3-70b",
146	            max_supported_concurrency=1024,
147	            endpoint_url="https://example.com",
148	            serving_framework="vLLM 0.4.0",
149	            **hw,
150	        )
151	
152	
153	def test_system_description_accepts_vcpu_without_core_count():
154	    hw = {k: v for k, v in _HW_FIELDS.items() if k != "host_processor_core_count"}
155	    sd = SystemDescription(
156	        division=Division.STANDARDIZED,
157	        publication_status=PublicationStatus.AVAILABLE,
158	        benchmark_model="llama3-70b",
159	        max_supported_concurrency=1024,
160	        endpoint_url="https://example.com",
161	        serving_framework="vLLM 0.4.0",
162	        host_processor_vcpu_count=40,
163	        **hw,
164	    )
165	    assert sd.host_processor_vcpu_count == 40
166	    assert sd.host_processor_core_count is None

168	
169	def test_system_description_allows_extra_fields():
170	    sd = SystemDescription(
171	        division=Division.RDI,
172	        publication_status=PublicationStatus.RDI,
173	        benchmark_model="test-model",
174	        max_supported_concurrency=64,
175	        endpoint_url="http://localhost",
176	        serving_framework="custom",
177	        extra_hw_detail="some extra info",  # allowed via extra="allow"
178	        **_HW_FIELDS,
179	    )
180	    assert sd.model_extra["extra_hw_detail"] == "some extra info"

183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# RunConfig
185	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	
187	
188	def _make_settings(
189	    concurrency: int, min_duration_ms: int = 600_000, stream_all_chunks: bool = True
190	) -> SettingsConfig:
191	    return SettingsConfig(
192	        load_pattern=LoadPatternConfig(type="concurrency", target_concurrency=concurrency),
193	        runtime=RuntimeConfig(min_duration_ms=min_duration_ms),
194	        client=ClientConfig(stream_all_chunks=stream_all_chunks),
195	    )
196	
197	
198	def test_measurement_point_config_valid():
199	    cfg = RunConfig(
200	        settings=_make_settings(64, 1_200_000),
201	        datasets=[{"name": "test-ds"}],
202	    )
203	    assert cfg.concurrency == 64
204	    assert cfg.dataset == "test-ds"
205	    assert cfg.settings.client.stream_all_chunks is True
206	
207	
208	def test_measurement_point_config_concurrency_computed():
209	    cfg = RunConfig(settings=_make_settings(128))
210	    assert cfg.concurrency == 128
211	
212	
213	def test_measurement_point_config_dataset_from_list():
214	    cfg = RunConfig(
215	        settings=_make_settings(32),
216	        datasets=[{"name": "mlperf-perf-dataset-v1"}, {"name": "other"}],
217	    )
218	    assert cfg.dataset == "mlperf-perf-dataset-v1"
219	
220	
221	def test_measurement_point_config_empty_datasets():
222	    cfg = RunConfig(settings=_make_settings(32))
223	    assert cfg.dataset == ""
224	
225	
226	def test_measurement_point_config_load_pattern_type_stored():
227	    cfg = RunConfig(settings=_make_settings(64))
228	    assert cfg.settings.load_pattern.type == "concurrency"
229	
230	
231	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	# RunSummary
233	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	
235	
236	def _make_summary(
237	    n_completed: int = 1000,
238	    n_issued: int = 1000,
239	    n_failed: int = 0,
240	    duration_ns: float = 600_000_000_000.0,
241	    total_tokens: float = 500_000.0,
242	    ttft_p50_ns: float = 150_000_000.0,
243	    ttft_p95_ns: float = 300_000_000.0,
244	) -> RunSummary:
245	    return RunSummary(
246	        n_samples_completed=n_completed,
247	        n_samples_issued=n_issued,
248	        n_samples_failed=n_failed,
249	        duration_ns=duration_ns,
250	        ttft=PercentileStats(
251	            total=ttft_p50_ns * n_completed, percentiles={"50": ttft_p50_ns, "95": ttft_p95_ns}
252	        ),
253	        output_sequence_lengths=PercentileStats(total=total_tokens),
254	    )
255	
256	
257	def test_point_result_summary_duration_ms():
258	    s = _make_summary(duration_ns=1_200_000_000_000.0)
259	    assert abs(s.duration_ms - 1_200_000.0) < 1.0
260	
261	
262	def test_point_result_summary_sample_count_alias():
263	    s = _make_summary(n_completed=512)
264	    assert s.sample_count == 512
265	
266	
267	def test_point_result_summary_system_tps():
268	    # 600 s run, 60000 tokens → 100 tok/s
269	    s = _make_summary(duration_ns=600_000_000_000.0, total_tokens=60_000.0)
270	    assert abs(s.system_tps - 100.0) < 0.01
271	
272	
273	def test_point_result_summary_ttft_ms_conversion():
274	    s = _make_summary(ttft_p50_ns=150_000_000.0, ttft_p95_ns=300_000_000.0)
275	    assert abs(s.ttft_p50_ms - 150.0) < 0.001
276	    assert abs(s.ttft_p95_ms - 300.0) < 0.001
277	
278	
279	def test_point_result_summary_total_output_tokens():
280	    s = _make_summary(total_tokens=22_407_098.0)
281	    assert s.total_output_tokens == 22_407_098
282	
283	
284	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	# AccuracyResult
286	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	
289	def test_accuracy_result_passed():
290	    ar = AccuracyResult(metric="rouge1", score=0.45, quality_target=0.43, passed=True)
291	    assert ar.passed
292	
293	
294	def test_accuracy_result_failed():
295	    ar = AccuracyResult(metric="rouge1", score=0.38, quality_target=0.43, passed=False)
296	    assert not ar.passed
297
```

```text
1	"""Tests for loader error paths (file-not-found, parse errors, validation errors)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	from unittest.mock import patch
7	
8	import yaml
9	
10	from submission_checker.loader import (
11	    load_accuracy_result,
12	    load_result_summary,
13	    load_run_config,
14	    load_system_description,
15	)
16	from submission_checker.models import Severity
17	
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	# load_system_description
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	
23	def test_load_system_description_missing_file(tmp_path):
24	    model, err = load_system_description(tmp_path / "missing.json")
25	    assert model is None
26	    assert "File not found" in err
27	
28	
29	def test_load_system_description_invalid_json(tmp_path):
30	    p = tmp_path / "bad.json"
31	    p.write_text("{not valid json")
32	    model, err = load_system_description(p)
33	    assert model is None
34	    assert "JSON parse error" in err
35	
36	
37	def test_load_system_description_schema_error(tmp_path):
38	    p = tmp_path / "bad.json"
39	    p.write_text(json.dumps({"division": "Standardized"}))  # missing required fields
40	    model, err = load_system_description(p)
41	    assert model is None
42	    assert "Validation error" in err
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# load_run_config — now returns (RunConfig | None, list[CheckResult])
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	
50	def test_load_run_config_missing_file(tmp_path):
51	    model, results = load_run_config(tmp_path / "missing.yaml")
52	    assert model is None
53	    assert len(results) == 1
54	    assert results[0].severity == Severity.ERROR
55	    assert "File not found" in results[0].message
56	
57	
58	def test_load_run_config_invalid_yaml(tmp_path):
59	    p = tmp_path / "bad.yaml"
60	    p.write_text("key: [unclosed")
61	    model, results = load_run_config(p)
62	    assert model is None
63	    assert len(results) == 1
64	    assert results[0].severity == Severity.ERROR
65	    assert "YAML parse error" in results[0].message
66	
67	
68	def test_load_run_config_non_dict_yaml(tmp_path):
69	    p = tmp_path / "list.yaml"
70	    p.write_text("- item1\n- item2\n")
71	    model, results = load_run_config(p)
72	    assert model is None
73	    assert len(results) == 1
74	    assert results[0].severity == Severity.ERROR
75	    assert "Expected a YAML mapping" in results[0].message
76	
77	
78	def test_load_run_config_schema_error(tmp_path):
79	    p = tmp_path / "bad.yaml"
80	    p.write_text(yaml.dump({"type": "submission"}))  # missing settings
81	    model, results = load_run_config(p)
82	    assert model is None
83	    assert len(results) == 1
84	    assert results[0].severity == Severity.ERROR
85	    assert "Validation error" in results[0].message
86	
87	
88	def test_load_run_config_valid_returns_check_results(tmp_path):
89	    p = tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"
90	    p.write_text(
91	        yaml.dump(
92	            {
93	                "settings": {
94	                    "load_pattern": {"type": "concurrency", "target_concurrency": 64},
95	                }
96	            }
97	        )
98	    )
99	    model, results = load_run_config(p, context={"yaml_path": p})
100	    assert model is not None
101	    # Should have validator-produced check results (load-pattern, streaming-config at minimum)
102	    rules = {r.rule for r in results}
103	    assert "load-pattern" in rules
104	    assert "streaming-config" in rules
105	
106	
107	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	# load_result_summary
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	
111	
112	def test_load_result_summary_missing_file(tmp_path):
113	    model, err = load_result_summary(tmp_path / "missing.json")
114	    assert model is None
115	    assert "File not found" in err
116	
117	
118	def test_load_result_summary_invalid_json(tmp_path):
119	    p = tmp_path / "bad.json"
120	    p.write_text("}")
121	    model, err = load_result_summary(p)
122	    assert model is None
123	    assert "JSON parse error" in err
124	
125	
126	def test_load_result_summary_schema_error(tmp_path):
127	    p = tmp_path / "bad.json"
128	    p.write_text(json.dumps({"n_samples_completed": "not-a-number"}))
129	    model, err = load_result_summary(p)
130	    assert model is None
131	    assert "Validation error" in err
132	
133	
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	# load_accuracy_result
136	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	
139	def test_load_accuracy_result_missing_file(tmp_path):
140	    model, err = load_accuracy_result(tmp_path / "missing.json")
141	    assert model is None
142	    assert "File not found" in err
143	
144	
145	def test_load_accuracy_result_schema_error(tmp_path):
146	    p = tmp_path / "bad.json"
147	    p.write_text(json.dumps({"metric": "rouge1"}))  # missing score, quality_target, passed
148	    model, err = load_accuracy_result(p)
149	    assert model is None
150	    assert "Validation error" in err
151	
152	
153	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
154	# OS-level I/O errors (covers loader.py lines 31-32 and 45-46)
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	
157	
158	def test_load_json_os_error(tmp_path):
159	    """_load_json must surface an OSError (e.g. permission denied) as an error message."""
160	    p = tmp_path / "ok.json"
161	    payload = {"metric": "rouge1", "score": 0.5, "quality_target": 0.43, "passed": True}
162	    p.write_text(json.dumps(payload))
163	    with patch("pathlib.Path.read_text", side_effect=OSError("permission denied")):
164	        model, err = load_accuracy_result(p)
165	    assert model is None
166	    assert "IO error" in err
167	
168	
169	def test_load_yaml_os_error(tmp_path):
170	    """_load_yaml must surface an OSError (e.g. permission denied) as an error message."""
171	    p = tmp_path / "run_64.yaml"
172	    lp = {"type": "concurrency", "target_concurrency": 64}
173	    p.write_text(yaml.dump({"settings": {"load_pattern": lp}}))
174	    with patch("pathlib.Path.read_text", side_effect=OSError("permission denied")):
175	        model, results = load_run_config(p)
176	    assert model is None
177	    assert len(results) == 1
178	    assert results[0].severity == Severity.ERROR
179	    assert "IO error" in results[0].message
180
```

Now I'll write a script to rename all test submission directories and update YAML content:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-14 15:17:49Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Convert and rename all test submission files from old format to new spec format

```bash
python3 - << 'EOF'
import os
import yaml
from pathlib import Path

BASE = Path("/home/arav/MLPerf/endpoints-submission-checker/src/submission_checker/test_submissions")

def convert_yaml(old_data: dict) -> dict:
    """Convert old nested format to new flat spec format."""
    settings = old_data.get("settings", {})
    lp = settings.get("load_pattern", {})
    runtime = settings.get("runtime", {})
    client = settings.get("client", {})
    datasets = old_data.get("datasets", [])
    
    concurrency = lp.get("target_concurrency", 0)
    dataset = datasets[0]["name"] if datasets else ""
    min_duration_ms = runtime.get("min_duration_ms", 600_000)
    stream_all_chunks = client.get("stream_all_chunks", True)
    
    return {
        "concurrency": concurrency,
        "dataset": dataset,
        "runtime_settings": {
            "load_pattern": "concurrency",
            "min_duration_ms": min_duration_ms,
            "stream_all_chunks": stream_all_chunks,
        }
    }

# Step 1: Rename results/run_N → results/point_N for all submissions
for run_dir in BASE.rglob("results/run_*"):
    if run_dir.is_dir():
        new_name = run_dir.parent / run_dir.name.replace("run_", "point_", 1)
        run_dir.rename(new_name)
        print(f"Renamed: {run_dir.relative_to(BASE)} → {new_name.name}")

# Step 2: Convert and rename run_*.yaml → point_*.yaml inside runs/ dirs
for runs_dir in BASE.rglob("runs"):
    if runs_dir.is_dir():
        for yaml_file in sorted(runs_dir.glob("run_*.yaml")):
            old_data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_file.read_text())
            new_data = convert_yaml(old_data)
            new_name = yaml_file.parent / yaml_file.name.replace("run_", "point_", 1)
            new_name.write_text(yaml.dump(new_data, default_flow_style=False))
            yaml_file.unlink()
            print(f"Converted: {yaml_file.name} → {new_name.name} (concurrency={new_data['concurrency']})")

# Step 3: Rename runs/ → points/
for runs_dir in sorted(BASE.rglob("runs"), reverse=True):
    if runs_dir.is_dir():
        new_dir = runs_dir.parent / "points"
        runs_dir.rename(new_dir)
        print(f"Renamed dir: {runs_dir.relative_to(BASE)} → points/")

print("Done!")
EOF

```

 `./src/structure.py`

```text
100	class ModelDir(BaseModel):
101	    """Validates points/, results/, and accuracy/ exist under a benchmark-model directory."""
102	
103	    _check_results: list[CheckResult] = PrivateAttr(default_factory=list)
104	
105	    root: Path
106	    system_id: str
107	    benchmark_model: str
108	
109	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
110	    @property
111	    def points_dir(self) -> Path:
112	        return self.root / "points"
113	
114	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
115	    @property
116	    def results_dir(self) -> Path:
117	        return self.root / "results"
118	
119	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
120	    @property
121	    def accuracy_dir(self) -> Path:
122	        return self.root / "accuracy"
123	
124	    @model_validator(mode="after")
125	    def _check_subdirs(self) -> ModelDir:
126	        for name in ("points", "results", "accuracy"):
127	            path = self.root / name
128	            if path.is_dir():
129	                self._check_results.append(
130	                    ok(
131	                        "pareto-subdir",
132	                        f"Found {name}/ in pareto/{self.system_id}/{self.benchmark_model}/",
133	                        path,
134	                        "#1",
135	                    )
136	                )
137	            else:
138	                self._check_results.append(
139	                    err(
140	                        "pareto-subdir",
141	                        f"Missing {name}/ in pareto/{self.system_id}/{self.benchmark_model}/",
142	                        path,
143	                        "#1",
144	                    )
145	                )
146	        return self
147
```
